          "text": "\n=== around Emotion behind control ===\n\nEmotion behind control.**\n   * Care and fear are present but wrapped inside procedure and analysis.\n   * She rarely names feelings directly; she describes their physical footprint and impact on reasoning.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nAnalytical, layered, more verbal than Jhace but still lean. Sentences can run longer, especially where she stacks conditions or tracks causality.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, \n\n=== around Sentence rules ===\n\nSentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n\n=== around Diction rules ===\n\nDiction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n   * Instrument status: lens clarity, hairlines in glass, alignment, scale marks.\n\n2. **Measurement / abstract sense**\n   * Subdivision counts, ranges, variance, repetition over Tides.\n   * She pairs sensory input with a mental number or range: \"within ± one subdivision.\"\n\n3. **Kinesthetic / touch**\n   * Hands on stone, instrumen\n\n=== around Lexicon ===\n\nLexicon & Global Constraints\n\nSame world constraints apply:\n\n* **No Earth units or references.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Quench, Anneal, spans, etc.\n\n* **No metaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adju\n\n=== around Metaphor ===\n\nmetaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine w\n\n=== around Pella ===\n\nPella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but stay controlled.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy\n\nWe want to keep her technical language consistent, but avoid distracting repetition in tight spans.\n\n### 8.1 Core measurement terms\n\nThese form a small controlled set:\n\n* **noise, signal, pattern, drift, deviation, shift, range, baseline, anomaly, state, source, external, internal, compounding, persistent.**\n\n**Rules:**\n\n* Within a single paragraph, avoid repeating the exact same keyword more than twice unless it's deliberate emphasis (e.g., everything revolves around noise vs signal in a short burst).\n* Over a scene, allow consistent reuse of key terms; she is a specialist with a tight lexicon.\n\n### 8.2 Instrument terms\n\nKeep technical clarity first; vary only where it doesn't confuse:\n\n* **Theodolite:** instrument, scope, tube, stand depending on context.\n* **Glass:** prism, pane, lens, plate, glass.\n* **Logging:** logbook, record, entry, column.\n\nDon't force synonyms that weaken the sense of a professio\n\n=== around Marisse ===\n\nMarisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse.                   "text": "\n=== around Emotion behind control ===\n\nEmotion behind control.**\n   * Care and fear are present but wrapped inside procedure and analysis.\n   * She rarely names feelings directly; she describes their physical footprint and impact on reasoning.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nAnalytical, layered, more verbal than Jhace but still lean. Sentences can run longer, especially where she stacks conditions or tracks causality.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, \n\n=== around Sentence rules ===\n\nSentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n\n=== around Diction rules ===\n\nDiction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n   * Instrument status: lens clarity, hairlines in glass, alignment, scale marks.\n\n2. **Measurement / abstract sense**\n   * Subdivision counts, ranges, variance, repetition over Tides.\n   * She pairs sensory input with a mental number or range: \"within ± one subdivision.\"\n\n3. **Kinesthetic / touch**\n   * Hands on stone, instrumen\n\n=== around Lexicon ===\n\nLexicon & Global Constraints\n\nSame world constraints apply:\n\n* **No Earth units or references.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Quench, Anneal, spans, etc.\n\n* **No metaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adju\n\n=== around Metaphor ===\n\nmetaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine w\n\n=== around Pella ===\n\nPella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but stay controlled.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy\n\nWe want to keep her technical language consistent, but avoid distracting repetition in tight spans.\n\n### 8.1 Core measurement terms\n\nThese form a small controlled set:\n\n* **noise, signal, pattern, drift, deviation, shift, range, baseline, anomaly, state, source, external, internal, compounding, persistent.**\n\n**Rules:**\n\n* Within a single paragraph, avoid repeating the exact same keyword more than twice unless it's deliberate emphasis (e.g., everything revolves around noise vs signal in a short burst).\n* Over a scene, allow consistent reuse of key terms; she is a specialist with a tight lexicon.\n\n### 8.2 Instrument terms\n\nKeep technical clarity first; vary only where it doesn't confuse:\n\n* **Theodolite:** instrument, scope, tube, stand depending on context.\n* **Glass:** prism, pane, lens, plate, glass.\n* **Logging:** logbook, record, entry, column.\n\nDon't force synonyms that weaken the sense of a professio\n\n=== around Marisse ===\n\nMarisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my \n"

    "54da98f8-cbb0-4928-b7f5-e53eaf3bd3e3": {
      "children": [

          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n   * Instrument status: lens clarity, hairlines in glass, alignment, scale marks.\n\n2. **Measurement / abstract sense**\n   * Subdivision counts, ranges, variance, repetition over Tides.\n   * She pairs sensory input with a mental number or range: \"within ± one subdivision.\"\n\n3. **Kinesthetic / touch**\n   * Hands on stone, instruments, glass.\n   * Physical confirmation: stand feet on stone, lack of grit, coolness/warmth.\n\n4. **Taste / internal sensation**\n   * Metallic taste as an early warning.\n   * Mouth, tongue, heartbeat, tension.\n\n5. **Sound**\n   * Humming glass, kiln exhale, city noise; used more as context and escalation than baseline.\n\n**Guideline:**\nIn important beats, pair the sensory detail with a measurement concept (e.g., \"Two subdivisions high and holding,\" \"small but compounding shift\").\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lexicon & Global Constraints\n\nSame world constraints apply:\n\n* **No Earth units or references.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Quench, Anneal, spans, etc.\n\n* **No metaphors\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765861275.1646073,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "# N01 – Jhace Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**\nThis document defines the *scene-level* and *sentence-level* voice for **Jhace POV** in N01, matching the tone, detail, and rhythm of Chapter 1: \"Something Wrong.\"\n\nUse this as the standard whenever writing or revising in Jhace's tight third-person POV.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace's mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   * He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   * Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   * Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his pri\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860417.9588027,
        "end_turn": null,

            "code": "jhace_voice = Path(\"/mnt/data/N01_Jhace_Prose_Voice_Guide.md\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\nprint(jhace_voice[:800])\n",
            "end_time": 1765860418.3047895,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,

                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "# N01 – Jhace Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**\nThis document defines the *scene-level* and *sentence-level* voice for **Jhace POV** in N01, matching the tone, detail, and rhythm of Chapter 1: \"Something Wrong.\"\n\nUse this as the standard whenever writing or revising in Jhace's tight third-person POV.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace's mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   * He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   * Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   * Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his pri\n"
                },

Core POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace's mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   * He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   * Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   * Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his pri\n",

    "5c8fecf7-6f88-4fcf-bd48-aca442ffacc9": {
      "children": [
        "adb9014c-8c8b-4e2d-a000-d8682538f336"
      ],
      "id": "5c8fecf7-6f88-4fcf-bd48-aca442ffacc9",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "001: ### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n002: \n003: **Function:**  \n004: Use when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n005: \n006: **Structure:**\n007: \n008: 1. **Baseline Observation**  \n009:    - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n010:    - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n011: \n012: 2. **Focused Attention**  \n013:    - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n014:    - Compares against prior experience.\n015: \n016: 3. **Testing / Probing**  \n017:    - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n018:    - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n019: \n020: 4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n021:    - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n022:    - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n023: \n024: 5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n025:    - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n026: \n027: **Emotional Arc:**  \n028: Curiosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n029: \n030: **Best Used When:**\n031: - Introducing a core problem.  \n032: - Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n033: \n034: **Forbidden Sequences:**\n035: - Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n036: - Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n037: \n038: **N01 Examples (intended):**\n039: - Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n040: - Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n041: \n042: ---\n043: \n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860370.3177607,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "5c8fecf7-6f88-4fcf-bd48-aca442ffacc9",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# print full beat_01 structure lines\nlines = beat[start:end].splitlines()\nfor i,l in enumerate(lines[:120]):\n    print(f\"{i+1:03d}: {l}\")\n",
            "end_time": 1765860370.621293,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "2e4b45d5-967a014b03b3f289ecb03e63_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "msg_type": "execute_input",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "2e4b45d5-967a014b03b3f289ecb03e63_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }
              },
              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "001: ### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n002: \n003: **Function:**  \n004: Use when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n005: \n006: **Structure:**\n007: \n008: 1. **Baseline Observation**  \n009:    - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n010:    - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n011: \n012: 2. **Focused Attention**  \n013:    - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n014:    - Compares against prior experience.\n015: \n016: 3. **Testing / Probing**  \n017:    - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n018:    - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n019: \n020: 4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n021:    - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n022:    - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n023: \n024: 5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n025:    - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n026: \n027: **Emotional Arc:**  \n028: Curiosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n029: \n030: **Best Used When:**\n031: - Introducing a core problem.  \n032: - Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n033: \n034: **Forbidden Sequences:**\n035: - Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n036: - Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n037: \n038: **N01 Examples (intended):**\n039: - Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n040: - Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n041: \n042: ---\n043: \n"
                },
                "msg_type": "stream",
                "parent_header": {

                  "version": "5.3"

            "messages": [
              {
                "message_type": "stream",
                "sender": "server",
                "stream_name": "stdout",
                "text": "001: ### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n002: \n003: **Function:**  \n004: Use when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n005: \n006: **Structure:**\n007: \n008: 1. **Baseline Observation**  \n009:    - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n010:    - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n011: \n012: 2. **Focused Attention**  \n013:    - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n014:    - Compares against prior experience.\n015: \n016: 3. **Testing / Probing**  \n017:    - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n018:    - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n019: \n020: 4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n021:    - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n022:    - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n023: \n024: 5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n025:    - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n026: \n027: **Emotional Arc:**  \n028: Curiosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n029: \n030: **Best Used When:**\n031: - Introducing a core problem.  \n032: - Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n033: \n034: **Forbidden Sequences:**\n035: - Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n036: - Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n037: \n038: **N01 Examples (intended):**\n039: - Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n040: - Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n041: \n042: ---\n043: \n",
                "time": 1765860370.560364
              }
            ],
            "run_id": "44978b5d-8dfc-4abe-9a6f-2647912182f9",
            "start_time": 1765860370.3177607,

I’ll look for clues on how to wrap things up smoothly, staying consistent with the earlier themes while keeping everything cohesive. Let’s continue!",

    "6404eabc-7553-4976-bb24-bafac8b3df6b": {
      "children": [
        "61a517ad-a489-4e01-9cd7-29cc6b1239d4"
      ],
      "id": "6404eabc-7553-4976-bb24-bafac8b3df6b",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "# N01 Story Bible — *The Grip That Holds* (GLASS / G)\n\n## SECTION 0: NOVELLA PARAMETERS\n\n- **Novella ID:** N01  \n- **Working Title:** *The Grip That Holds*  \n- **Turning:** 1 — **GLASS** (fragile formation, first awakening; transparent but breakable)  \n- **Acrostic Letter:** **G** (from “Grip”; leading article ignored)  \n- **Acrostic Position:** **1/5** in Turning 1  \n- **Structure:** **12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue = 15 segments**  \n- **Word Count Target:** ~18,000–21,000 words  \n- **Chapter Target Range:** 1,200–2,200 words (locked segments may exceed for beat needs)  \n- **Timeline Covered:** **Tide 0 (Kindling→Quench) through ~Tide 20** post-Piercing  \n- **Saga Position:** Novella **1 of 30**  \n- **Knowledge Frame:** **INNOCENT** (no conscious divine awareness on-page)\n\n### Bible Version History\n- **v1.0:** Initial complete N01 bible generated from locked grid + beat mapping layer + core canon files.\n\n### Acrostic Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- Turning 1 word: **GLASS** → N01 supplies **G** via “Grip.” **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 1: SAGA STRUCTURE CONTEXT\n\n### Fixed Elements (All Novellas)\n- 15 segments total (12 chapters, 2 interludes, 1 epilogue)\n- Soulpulse always has **visible physical cost** (strain → fatigue → burnout → collapse; indirect death risk)\n- Knowledge-gate discipline: **truth reveals only when scheduled**\n- Beat Unpredictability tracking: record **Saga Beat IDs** to prevent repetition across later novellas\n\n### Variable Elements (This Novella)\n\n**Turning Context**\n- **Current Turning:** 1/6 (GLASS)  \n- **Turning Theme:** fragile bonds forming under pressure; learning what “holding” costs  \n- **Place in Turning:** 1/5  \n- **Completed Turnings:** none  \n- **Remaining Turnings:** CRACK, FORGE, SMELT, CLEAR, BREAK\n\n**Narrative Focus**\n- The Piercing’s exit-side catastrophe hits Saint Merro; two craft-minds converge:\n  - **Jhace** (Underface structural-glass worker) discovers Soulpulse through necessity.\n  - **Marisse** (observatory measurer / pattern-mapper) forces method onto chaos.\n- Romance forms as a *systems bond*: “you keep me from breaking; I keep us from lying to ourselves.”\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 2: PRIOR NOVELLAS SUMMARY\n- **None.** N01 is saga entry point.\n\n### Continuity Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- No prior-state dependencies to reconcile. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 3: KNOWLEDGE-GATE COMPLIANCE\n\n### Authoritative Canon References\n- `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md` (immutable physical consequences)  \n- `02-Soulpulse-Core.md` (true mechanics + cost ladder; touch-dominant early)  \n- `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md` (what can be known in Turning 1)  \n- `10-World-TeOga.md` (Saint Merro, Underface, exit scar context)  \n- `11-Factions-Politics.md` (Guild Compact, Church blocs, RRO pressure patterns)  \n- `N01_Chapter_Grid_12ch_REVISED.md` (mandatory plot/structure reference)  \n- `N01_Beat_Mapping_Layer_FINAL.md` (local beat IDs + saga beat logging)\n\n### What May Be Revealed On-Page in N01 (Allowed)\n- The Piercing happened; the world is unstable; “resonance/Soulpulse” is appearing.\n- Soulpulse in early Turning 1 is **touch-reliant**, emotion-triggered, and **hurts**.\n- Institutions are confused, reactive, and opportunistic (registration, suppression, “ownership” attempts).\n\n### Forbidden / Locked (Do NOT Reveal On-Page in N01)\n- Explicit god names, true god-vessel identities, cosmology-level causation, and late-saga mechanics.\n- Any direct explanation of Principles as divine masks; any “purpose of the Piercing” certainty.\n\n### Compliance Plan\n- Interludes show **biased perception** (Form / Purity lenses) as *strange cognition*, **not divinity**.\n- Any behind-the-scenes truth remains tagged **LOCKED** in this bible section only.\n\n### Knowledge-Gate Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- N01 reveals “power exists + cost is real,” not “what it ultimately is.” **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 4: BEAT MAPPING + UNPREDICTABILITY CONTROL\n\n### Beat System Rule\n- **Prose + validators:** use **N01 Local Beats (BEAT_01–BEAT_08)**  \n- **Saga tracking:** log **Saga Beat ID** per segment\n\n### N01 Segment Beat Assignments (Authoritative: Beat Mapping Layer)\n| Segment | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat ID |\n|---|---|---:|---:|\n| Ch1 | Something Wrong | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 |\n| Ch2 | Glass That Listens | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 |\n| Ch3 | The Sky Breaks | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 |\n| Ch4 | After the Shudder | BEAT_02 | Beat 1 |\n| Ch5 | Lines That Don’t Lie | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 |\n| Ch6 | Weight That Won’t Yield | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 |\n| Ch7 | Quiet Calculations | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 |\n| **Interlude I** | Corlexi — System vs Chaos | (Interlude: Build-Up) | (Log as Build-Up) |\n| Ch8 | Proof and Resistance | BEAT_04 | Beat 16 |\n| Ch9 | Ash and Claims | BEAT_07 | Beat 26 |\n| Ch10 | Near Collapse | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 |\n| **Interlude II** | Mobel — Purity Rising | (Interlude: Build-Up) | (Log as Build-Up) |\n| Ch11 | Holding Together | BEAT_08 | Beat 17 |\n| Ch12 | Choosing the Weight | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 |\n| **Epilogue** | *(N01 hook)* | (Epilogue: Hook) | (Log as Hook) |\n\n**Opening exception:** Ch1→Ch2 repeats BEAT_01 intentionally (paired mirror opening). Allowed per mapping notes.\n\n### Beat Pattern Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- No forbidden short-cycle repetition beyond the approved opening mirror. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 5: INTERLUDE PLACEMENT\n\n- **Interlude I:** after **Chapter 7** (breathing space; institutional/divine lens enters)  \n- **Interlude II:** after **Chapter 10** (between near-collapse and resolution; second lens enters)  \n- **Minimum separation satisfied:** Ch7 → I → Ch8 → Ch9 → Ch10 → I → Ch11\n\n### INTERLUDE I — Corlexi: System vs Chaos (Build-Up)\n- Location: Crown administrative chambers + observation points  \n- Function: introduce *Form-biased perception* as an ordering hunger; foreshadow pressure apparatus\n\n### INTERLUDE II — Mobel: Purity Rising (Build-Up)\n- Location: entry-side (opposite hemisphere)  \n- Function: introduce *Purity-biased perception* as cleansing judgment; foreshadow ideological collision\n\n### Interlude Placement Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- Placement meets structural constraints and strengthens Act transitions. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 6: CHAPTER + INTERLUDE + EPILOGUE SUMMARIES  \n*(100–300 words per segment; prose-facing knowledge only)*\n\n### CHAPTER 1 — “Something Wrong”\nJhace works the Underface by feel—load, hum, stress—catching a wrong note in stone and glass that refuses to resolve. Bram needles him for working alone, but the teasing sits on an older truth: Jhace trusts structures more than people. Above, the city’s supports feel tired; below, water and grit talk too loudly. The Tide’s light shifts toward Kindling’s end and the Underface grows tense—workers rushing, voices sharpened. Jhace marks cracks, makes tiny repairs that should be enough, then finds that “enough” won’t hold. A tremor rolls through, not the worst, but wrong in rhythm. He climbs toward street level, trying to place the pattern in his mind. The chapter ends with him hearing the city like a living thing—holding its breath.\n\n### CHAPTER 2 — “Glass That Listens”\nMarisse measures the sky as if measurement can keep it honest. On an observatory platform at Saint Merro’s rim, she tracks distortions and pressure shifts with obsessive vigilance. Pella—grounded, practical—keeps her fed, warm, and tethered to the present without insulting her fear. Marisse’s trauma surfaces as ritual: re-check, re-check, re-check. The air’s refraction bends in ways her instruments can’t comfortably name, and the horizon looks too thin. As the Tide moves toward Pouring, the sky’s line fractures—first as a subtle wrongness, then as an event. Marisse catches the first usable data of the world changing and realizes the numbers are not just “bad.” They’re unprecedented. The exit begins.\n\n### CHAPTER 3 — “The Sky Breaks”\nCatastrophe arrives mid-breath. In the Underface tunnel, a wall moves before Jhace can decide to move; the city becomes weight without permission. Chloen is trapped. Jhace’s old ledger of names—those he couldn’t hold—spikes through him as panic. He braces a beam with his body, then something answers the desperation: copper-gold glow floods his palms and runs into the structure like heat finding a seam. The beam holds—barely. He uses it again at street level, pressing both hands to a failing support while the building’s stress-lines reveal themselves like veins of light. The cost hits hard: tremor, pain, heat-lash up his arms, a collapse to his knees. In the chaos, Marisse appears—not as comfort, but as demand: she needs to know what happened, how, and what it cost. Their first exchange is competence colliding with exhaustion.\n\n### CHAPTER 4 — “After the Shudder”\nThe first hours after the exit are triage and lies people tell themselves to keep moving. Jhace tries to repeat what he did, but the power doesn’t obey pride—only need, contact, and a protective impulse he can barely control. He fails once, shame and panic rising with the wall’s groan, then succeeds on a second attempt when he stops trying to “perform” and simply holds. The glow threads into damaged masonry; the structure steadies long enough for bodies to be pulled free. Marisse watches with a scientist’s hunger and a survivor’s fear, tracking every condition: where he touches, what he feels, how fast the tremor arrives. She’s disturbed by how intimate the mechanism seems—how emotional. Jhace is disturbed by how quickly the cost accumulates. They leave the site with a fragile agreement: she will document; he will try again—because not trying means watching more names get added.\n\n### CHAPTER 5 — “Lines That Don’t Lie”\nMarisse turns the ruined streets into a ledger. She maps holds, collapses, and “near-holds,” marking where Jhace’s copper-gold imprint appears and where it doesn’t. Pressure arrives from both directions: the RRO wants to institutionalize what she’s seeing; guild factors want to monetize it or steer it away from “unprofitable” neighborhoods. She refuses both—too many bodies were left under tidy decisions. Pella supports the refusal with care that doesn’t soften the line: food, heat, rest, the reminder that conviction still needs a body. From a distance, Marisse watches Jhace deteriorate—tremor baseline, pallor, stubbornness. The chapter ends with her deciding observation isn’t enough. If the power is going to keep being used, it needs method before it kills him.\n\n### CHAPTER 6 — “Weight That Won’t Yield”\nA larger hold begins to fail—an arch network that will cascade if one segment drops. Jhace arrives first, already shaking, and learns that heroics are just another way to die. Marisse forces a plan: contact points, sequence, pressure relief, and the smallest possible pulse. Jhace resents the constraint until he realizes the constraint is what lets him stand back up. He presses his hands to stone at her count, the glow threading in controlled paths instead of flooding. The arch holds—narrowly. The cost still hits (heat, tremor, nausea), but it’s less catastrophic than before. In the aftermath, they argue: he wants to keep moving; she wants to record and ration. The argument doesn’t break them—it defines their partnership: she won’t let him burn out for pride; he won’t let her hide in numbers when people are screaming.\n\n### CHAPTER 7 — “Quiet Calculations”\nThey retreat to a temporary calm—quiet not because the world is safe, but because exhaustion forces stillness. Marisse lays out her maps; Jhace lays out his hands, staring at the faint after-lines under his skin. They name boundaries without naming feelings: how many holds per Tide, where to stop, what signs mean “no more.” Pella and Bram appear as mirrors—care and craft, both insisting that “holding” includes the holder. The intimacy is not sudden romance; it’s shared breath after shared terror. Jhace admits he’s afraid of becoming a tool. Marisse admits she’s afraid of being wrong when wrongness costs lives. They choose to keep working together anyway, not because it’s easy, but because alone they will fail faster.\n\n### INTERLUDE I — “Corlexi: System vs Chaos”\nCorlexi moves through administrative chambers like a man trying to keep his eyes from seeing too much. He experiences the city’s ruin as misalignment—systems out of true, geometry offended, hierarchy collapsing into noise. He does not call it divinity; he calls it stress. Reports cross his desk: “resonants,” copper-gold stabilization, uncontrolled costs. He wants rules, registries, proofs—anything to turn chaos into process. Yet something in him rejects the mess with visceral disgust, as if disorder were a personal insult. He drafts a “compliance trial” proposal and tells himself it is for public safety. The interlude ends with Corlexi deciding to observe the rumored stabilizer directly—because if the city is going to be held, it must be held *correctly*.\n\n### CHAPTER 8 — “Proof and Resistance”\nJhace pushes back against being managed. In his forge, he frames Marisse’s methodology as control, an intrusion into the one place he still feels competent. Marisse refuses to soothe him. Stone doesn’t lie, she says—neither do his tremors. Their conflict sharpens into honesty: he’s terrified she’ll reduce him to a diagram; she’s terrified he’ll die to prove he can do it alone. She proposes a small test—controlled contact, minimal pulse, measured outcome—so they can separate “miracle” from repeatable method. The test works. The glow follows targeted stress-lines and fades cleanly. Jhace still pays (heat, shaking), but the payment is smaller, survivable. The chapter ends with Jhace conceding the truth he hates: she makes him better at holding. And Marisse concedes the truth she fears: she cares enough to fight him for it.\n\n### CHAPTER 9 — “Ash and Claims”\nThe streets get political. Corvin and Tellis offer resources—access, guards, material—at the price of ownership: registration, exclusive deployment, the right to decide which districts deserve holding. Marisse is tempted; safety is seductive when the ground won’t stop moving. She asks Jhace for input for the first time instead of assuming she’s right. His question is simple and brutal: what do *you* want, underneath the fear? Marisse admits her contradictions: she wants safety, recognition, and to stay clean of complicity—things that don’t fit together. Jhace chooses her anyway, not as a romantic flourish, but as a shared risk: if they take the offer, they become a weapon. They refuse publicly. The refusal makes enemies—and makes their bond real.\n\n### CHAPTER 10 — “Near Collapse”\nA major support line threatens to go—too many weakened buildings leaning into one another like drunks. Jhace and Marisse arrive to a crowd, shouting, panic, and officials trying to seize control. They coordinate anyway: she clears space and calls sequence; he makes contact where it matters most. The pulse is stronger than planned because the structure is worse than reported. Copper-gold floods, revealing a terrifying web of stress; Jhace holds it long enough for evacuation and shoring—but the cost spikes toward the next threshold: tremor becomes full-body shaking, vision narrowing, pain blooming through his arms and chest. He nearly collapses in the open. Marisse catches him before he hits stone, furious and frightened in equal measure. They escape the site with the hold intact and Jhace barely conscious, the victory tasting like ash.\n\n### INTERLUDE II — “Mobel: Purity Rising”\nOn the entry-side, Mobel lives inside pain. A shard in his leg becomes a teacher: every throb is a lesson in separation—what should remain and what must be cut away. He views the world through a sharpening lens, judging without intending to: weak structures, weak people, weak doctrine. He does not name it Purity; he names it clarity. In a moment of stillness, he becomes aware that *something else* is moving—another pressure, another intent—not here, but across the world’s curve. The awareness is not a conversation, just a clash of directional certainty. Mobel decides that whatever is happening, it must be cleansed into an answer. The interlude ends with him stepping toward authority, not as a servant, but as a blade seeking a handle.\n\n### CHAPTER 11 — “Holding Together”\nAfter near-collapse, the relationship stops pretending it’s only work. Jhace’s recovery is ugly: shaking, nausea, pain-laced sleep, the fear that the next pulse will take something permanent. Marisse is forced into caretaking without losing her edge; Pella and Bram support without crowding. In the quiet, Jhace admits he can’t keep doing this without her—without the method, and without the human anchor. Marisse admits she can’t keep treating him as a data source without becoming a monster. They become lovers not as reward, but as a pact: if they’re going to risk their bodies and reputations, they won’t do it half-alive. They set rules for cost management and agree to face the political consequences together.\n\n### CHAPTER 12 — “Choosing the Weight”\nThe city doesn’t stabilize; it only pauses between harms. Jhace and Marisse review their maps, their limits, and the enemies they’ve made. They choose a path forward: keep the work public enough to prevent quiet capture, but disciplined enough to survive. They plan a “holding circuit” with rotation, witnesses, and documentation—method as protection. There is tenderness, but it’s threaded through dread: another Tide of tremors could break everything again. Their final act is not a grand rescue; it’s a decision to keep showing up with eyes open, accepting that holding costs, and that love doesn’t erase cost—it makes the payment worth naming. The chapter ends with them stepping back into the streets as a unit: not safe, not certain, but aligned.\n\n### EPILOGUE — “A Ledger Opens” *(hook; ~900 words target)*\nA quiet room. A file begins. Someone with institutional power—careful hands, careful language—compiles the first real ledger of “resonant stabilization.” Names are recorded alongside locations, effects, and cost symptoms. The report frames the phenomenon as a resource and a threat. A line is drawn between “public safety” and “unlicensed interference.” Outside the window, Saint Merro creaks; inside, policy is forged from fear. The epilogue ends on a simple truth: Jhace and Marisse believe they chose each other and the work—but now the work has been noticed, and notice has teeth.\n\n### Chapter Summary Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- 15 segments covered; summaries stay within N01 knowledge frame. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 7: CHARACTER STATE TABLE (END OF N01)\n\n### Current Status Tracking (Major)\n| Character | Age | Status | Cost Level | Power Level | Knowledge State | Faction | Relationship Status | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **Jhace Torrin** | Adult | Alive; physically strained | Fatigue→near Burnout risk (post Ch10) | Early Turning-1 touch stabilization | Knows “it’s real + it hurts”; no origin | Guild-adjacent (craft) | Bonded to Marisse | Baseline tremor established; pride is a threat |\n| **Marisse** | Adult | Alive; stable | None (observer) | Non-user (in N01) | Method-first understanding; no origin | Observatory / independent | Partner→lover with Jhace | Refuses capture by RRO or guild factors |\n| **Pella** | Adult | Alive | None | Non-user | Practical awareness only | Civilian | Ally to Marisse | Care network anchor |\n| **Bram** | Adult | Alive | None | Non-user | Practical awareness only | Craft circle | Ally to Jhace | Reinforces craft + limits |\n| **Chloen** | Adult | Alive (rescued) | Trauma | Non-user | Survivor knowledge only | Civilian | N/A | Catalyst for Jhace’s first pulse |\n| **Corvin / Tellis** | Adults | Alive | None | Unknown/none | Institutional framing | Authority / guild-political | Antagonistic pressure | Attempt to control access |\n| **Corlexi** | Adult | Alive | None (on-page) | (LOCKED) | “Safety + systems” lens | Administrative bloc | N/A | Interlude: ordering hunger (no self-knowledge) |\n| **Mobel** | Adult | Alive; injured | Pain baseline | (LOCKED) | “clarity/judgment” lens | Entry-side authority sphere | N/A | Interlude: rising severity (no self-knowledge) |\n\n### God-Vessel Identity Tracking (LOCKED — author-only)\n- **Corlexi:** Form-aligned vessel (identity masked; not conscious)  \n- **Mobel:** Purity-aligned vessel (identity masked; not conscious)\n\n### Territorial Control (as-of end N01)\n- **Saint Merro (exit-side city):** contested influence — Guild Compact infrastructure power + reactive authority blocs + emergent RRO pressure\n- **Underface:** craft-controlled, vulnerable to raids/registration\n\n### Character State Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- End-states match chapter grid progression (Stage 1 baseline; near-threshold at Ch10). **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 8: SETTING + WORLD STATE (N01)\n\n### Primary Locations\n- **Saint Merro (Te’Oga’s Crown)**: layered harbor city hit hardest by exit-side catastrophe  \n- **The Underface:** sub-street arches, culverts, maintenance corridors; Jhace’s forge and the city’s “belly”  \n- **Observatory platform:** measurement vantage at the rim  \n- **Glass Quarter refugee camp:** displacement, political leverage, moral pressure sites  \n- **Crown administrative chambers:** policy response + quiet capture attempts\n\n### Environmental Rules (post-Piercing)\n- Repeated tremors; unstable supports; water intrusion; fused glass/metal debris near the scar  \n- Public fear + opportunism; “safety” used as justification for control\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 9: SOULPULSE EXECUTION PLAN (N01)\n\n### Usage Count + Placement (from grid)\n- **Total confirmed uses:** 7  \n  - Ch3: 2 (beam hold; building support)  \n  - Ch4: 1 + 1 failed attempt (triage wall)  \n  - Ch6: 1 (arch coordination)  \n  - Ch8: 1 (controlled efficiency test)  \n  - Ch10: 1 (major support; near-threshold)\n\n### Rules Enforced On-Page\n- Early Turning 1: **touch required**; emotion trigger must be credible (protective need)  \n- **Visible costs:** heat, tremor, pain, exhaustion; near-collapse by Ch10  \n- Recovery requires rest and care; no “free casting”\n\n### Soulpulse Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- Costs escalate; no costless miracles; no forbidden capabilities. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 10: ACT / STRUCTURE MAP (N01)\n\n- **Act I — The Cut (Ch1–Ch4):** crisis + first Soulpulse + first contact  \n- **Act II — The Pattern (Ch5–Ch7 + Interlude I):** method emerges; partnership stabilizes  \n- **Act III — The Claim (Ch8–Ch12 + Interlude II + Epilogue):** ownership pressure + near-collapse + commitment + hook\n\n### Structural Compliance Checkpoint ✓\n- 12 chapters + 2 interludes + epilogue; interludes within placement band and separated. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 11: FORESHADOWING + FORBIDDEN KNOWLEDGE CONTROL\n\n### Allowed Seeds (non-revealing)\n- “Form vs Purity” as *felt bias* (order-hunger vs cleansing-judgment), never named as gods\n- Institutional capture language (“registration,” “compliance,” “licensed deployment”)\n- Hints that Soulpulse has repeatable conditions\n\n### Forbidden Seeds (do not include)\n- True divine names, cosmology mechanics, explicit vessel confirmation, endgame revelations\n\n### Forbidden Knowledge Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- Seeds remain interpretive; no direct reveals. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 12: EMOTIONAL ARC MAP\n\n### Chapter-by-Chapter Intensity\n- Ch1 **Low**  \n- Ch2 **Low**  \n- Ch3 **High**  \n- Ch4 **Medium**  \n- Ch5 **Medium**  \n- Ch6 **Medium-High**  \n- Ch7 **Low**  \n- Interlude I **Build-Up**  \n- Ch8 **Medium**  \n- Ch9 **Medium**  \n- Ch10 **High**  \n- Interlude II **Build-Up**  \n- Ch11 **High**  \n- Ch12 **Medium**  \n- Epilogue **Tense / Quiet Threat**\n\n### Turning-Specific Arc (GLASS)\n- Fragile formation → first stress-lines → deliberate reinforcement (relationship as structure)\n\n### Emotional Arc Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- Variance achieved; peaks at Ch3/Ch10/Ch11; resolves to chosen burden. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 13: DELIVERABLE PROTOCOL\n\n- **Internal naming:** `N01_Story_Bible_v1_0.md`  \n- **Prose handoff requirements met:** beats logged (local + saga), interludes placed, Soulpulse costs tracked, knowledge gates enforced.\n\n### Deliverable Verification Checkpoint ✓\n- All required bible sections present and filled for N01 production. **PASS**\n\n---\n\n## SECTION 14: POST-GENERATION VERIFICATION (SUMMARY)\n\n- **Structural:** 15 segments planned; interludes correctly placed. ✓  \n- **Style / Lexicon:** Te’Oga time units used (Tide, Kindling/Pouring/Shaping/Striking/Quench). ✓  \n- **Canon:** Piercing aftermath consistent; early Soulpulse touch + cost consistent. ✓  \n- **Beats:** Local beats align to mapping layer; saga beats logged. ✓  \n- **Knowledge gates:** no premature divinity/cosmology reveals. ✓  \n\n**STATUS:** **COMPLETE — N01 Bible v1.0**"
          ]

          "text": "# 50 â€“ LEXICON & STYLE GUIDE\n**Prose Rules and Measurement Usage for Confluence Chronicles**\n\n---\n\n## 1. DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis guide defines how the story is written on the page: narrative voice, formatting, language constraints, and how the Te'Oga measurement systems are used in prose.  \nIt exists to keep all 30 novellas consistent in tone, clarity, and in-world terminology.\n\n---\n\n## 2. PROSE LAWS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)\n\n### 2.1 Point of View\n\n- Camera stays inside their sensory and conceptual limitsâ€”no omniscient commentary in main chapters.  \n\n### 2.2 Tense\n\n- Default: **tight past tense** for main chapters.  \n- Short present-tense flashes allowed only for immediate, high-stress sensations (pain, impact, realization), and must be brief.\n\n### 2.3 Language Constraints\n\n- **No metaphors or similes** in main chapters.  \n  - Do not write \"like\" / \"as if\" comparisons for imagery.  \n  - Describe what is literally present: light, sound, motion, weight, temperature, stress.  \n- Descriptions must be **plain, concrete, and physical**.  \n  - \"The beam bowed two fingers under the load\" is correct.  \n  - \"The beam sagged like an old man\" is not.\n\n### 2.4 Magic Description (Grounded and Anime-Escalated)\n\n- Soulpulse use is always described through **material effects and bodily cost**, not abstract energy:\n  - Show beams straightening, glass knitting, joints locking, stone crumbling.  \n  - Show shaking hands, shortness of breath, pain, exhaustion.  \n- Even at late-saga anime levels:\n  - No color-coded auras (no \"blue aura of Purity,\" etc.).  \n  - No vague \"energy blasts\" without specifying pressure, shrapnel, heat, concussion, or structural result.  \n  - If someone \"flies,\" it must be grounded (platforms of locked debris, wind pressure control, etc.).\n\n### 2.5 Cost Signaling\n\nEvery on-page use of Soulpulse must show **some** cost, scaled to intensity:\n\n- Light use: tremor, tension, extra breathing.  \n- Heavy use: shaking limbs, partial collapse, muddled thoughts.  \n- Fathom Break / god-tier: near-total collapse, lasting injury.\n\nThere is no cost-free use of Soulpulse. If it looks effortless, the pain is deferred, not erased.\n\n---\n\n## 3. MEASUREMENT LEXICON USAGE\n\nAll in-world measurement uses the Te'Oga systems: time, distance, and mass.  \nMetric/imperial terms (meters, miles, kilograms, seconds, weeks) **never** appear in character-facing prose.\n\n### 3.1 Time Units\n\nCanonical units:\n\n- **Tide** â€“ day (~24 hours)  \n- **Anneal** â€“ 5 Tides (week)  \n- **Crucible** â€“ \n"
        },

DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis guide defines how the story is written on the page: narrative voice, formatting, language constraints, and how the Te'Oga measurement systems are used in prose.  \nIt exists to keep all 30 novellas consistent in tone, clarity, and in-world terminology.\n\n---\n\n## 2. PROSE LAWS (NON-NEGOTIABLE)\n\n### 2.1 Point of View\n\n- Camera stays inside their sensory and conceptual limitsâ€”no omniscient commentary in main chapters.  \n\n### 2.2 Tense\n\n- Default: **tight past tense** for main chapters.  \n- Short present-tense flashes allowed only for immediate, high-stress sensations (pain, impact, realization), and must be brief.\n\n### 2.3 Language Constraints\n\n- **No metaphors or similes** in main chapters.  \n  - Do not write \"like\" / \"as if\" comparisons for imagery.  \n  - Describe what is literally present: light, sound, motion, weight, temperature, stress.  \n- Descriptions must be **plain, concrete, and physical**.  \n  - \"The beam bowed two fingers under the load\" is correct.  \n  - \"The beam sagged like an old man\" is not.\n\n### 2.4 Magic Description (Grounded and Anime-Escalated)\n\n- Soulpulse use is always described through **material effects and bodily cost**, not abstract energy:\n  - Show beams straightening, glass knitting, joints locking, stone crumbling.  \n  - Show shaking hands, shortness of breath, pain, exhaustion.  \n- Even at late-saga anime levels:\n  - No color-coded auras (no \"blue aura of Purity,\" etc.).  \n  - No vague \"energy blasts\" without specifying pressure, shrapnel, heat, concussion, or structural result.  \n  - If someone \"flies,\" it must be grounded (platforms of locked debris, wind pressure control, etc.).\n\n### 2.5 Cost Signaling\n\nEvery on-page use of Soulpulse must show **some** cost, scaled to intensity:\n\n- Light use: tremor, tension, extra breathing.  \n- Heavy use: shaking limbs, partial collapse, muddled thoughts.  \n- Fathom Break / god-tier: near-total collapse, lasting injury.\n\nThere is no cost-free use of Soulpulse. If it looks effortless, the pain is deferred, not erased.\n\n---\n\n## 3. MEASUREMENT LEXICON USAGE\n\nAll in-world measurement uses the Te'Oga systems: time, distance, and mass.  \nMetric/imperial terms (meters, miles, kilograms, seconds, weeks) **never** appear in character-facing prose.\n\n### 3.1 Time Units\n\nCanonical units:\n\n- **Tide** â€“ day (~24 hours)  \n- **Anneal** â€“ 5 Tides (week)  \n- **Crucible** â€“ \n"

          "text": "import pathlib, textwrap, re, json, os, pandas as pd\npaths = [\n    \"/mnt/data/N01_Tiffani_Prose_Voice_Guide.md\",\n    \"/mnt/data/N01_Jhace_Prose_Voice_Guide.md\",\n    \"/mnt/data/Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md\",\n    \"/mnt/data/OmniMeta_Saga_Story_Bible.md\",\n]\nfor p in paths:\n    print(\"\\n---\", os.path.basename(p), \"---\")\n    print(pathlib.Path(p).read_text()[:1200])\n"

          "text": "### **INTERLUDE I: \"Corlexi - System vs Chaos\"**\n- **POV:** Corlexi (Form god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~6-7\n- **Setting:** Crown administrative chambers, multiple observation points\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm technique (god-vessel perception)\n- Principle bias manifestation (everything seen through Form lens)\n- Innocent frame maintained (no recognition of divinity)\n\n**Purpose:** Establish divine forces moving, show limitation of single-Principle perspective, foreshadow institutional pressure in Act III\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },

          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "ng walls, marginal foundationsâ€”hit hardest by the exit event.  \n  - **The Underface:** Sub-street level of arches, culverts, and maintenance corridors; Jhaceâ€™s forge is here.\n\n**Post-Piercing Condition**\n\n- Exit scar crater and rubble ring sit just beyond the Crownâ€™s urban line:\n  - Crater: ~50 cables across at the rim, thermally active, part of it visible from high buildings.  \n  - Rubble ring: unstable stone and fused glass/metal, partly quarantined by guild decree.  \n- Saint Merro: Many buildings leaning or collapsed after quakes; improvised shoring, rope stays, and emergency braces everywhere.\n\n**Role in Saga**\n\n- Primary setting for N01â€“N05:\n  - Jhaceâ€™s forge (Underface, two cables below main streets).  \n- Later Turnings: Becomes symbol of â€œwhat can be heldâ€ after repeated stabilization and reconstruction attempts.\n\n---\n\n### 3.2 Entry-Side Coast & Harbor City (Mobelâ€™s Ground)\n\n**Location & Layout**\n\n- On the opposite hemisphere, near the northwestern coast where th\n"
        },

    "6bb0bc7d-d918-4dda-872c-d743d87493d8": {
      "children": [
        "e8ddc0c6-acc4-4aa9-a7ba-f7ee74e0c697"
      ],
      "id": "6bb0bc7d-d918-4dda-872c-d743d87493d8",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE\n\nThis document translates between:\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\n\n**Rule of Precedence:**\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\n\n---\n\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\n\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External Threat → Narrow Escape |\n| **BEAT_04** | Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | **Beat 16** | Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)* |\n| **BEAT_05** | Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe | **Beat 13** | Teaching Moment → Student Failure → Lesson Learned *(or Beat 20 if student-success weighted)* |\n| **BEAT_06** | Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate |\n| **BEAT_07** | Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand | **Beat 26** | Faction Confrontation → Ideological Debate → Stalemate *(institutional confrontation analog)* |\n| **BEAT_08** | Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment | **Beat 17** | Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection Deepened *(or Beat 21 if reflection/purpose weighted, minimal intimacy)* |\n\n---\n\n## SPECIAL CASE MAPPINGS\n\n### **BEAT_04 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)**\n- Use when: Character investigates, discovers truth, truth creates choice/complication\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 28 (Moral Compromise → Guilt → Justification)**\n- Use when: Offer requires ethical compromise, character feels moral weight, accepts or refuses with consequences\n- Example: If BEAT_04 chapter centers on moral pressure rather than investigation\n\n**Logging Guideline:** Default to Beat 16 unless moral compromise is central theme.\n\n---\n\n### **BEAT_08 Context-Dependent Mapping:**\n\n**Primary Mapping: Beat 17 (Intimacy → Vulnerability → Connection)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes emotional vulnerability, sharing trauma, relationship deepening\n- Example: Ch 11 (trauma exchange, mutual need acknowledged)\n\n**Alternative Mapping: Beat 21 (Rest → Reflection → Renewed Purpose)**\n- Use when: Chapter emphasizes rest, reflection, renewed commitment with minimal vulnerability\n- Example: Ch 7 (data compilation), Ch 12 (writing priorities on forge wall)\n\n**Logging Guideline:** If intimacy/vulnerability present → Beat 17. If primarily rest/reflection → Beat 21.\n\n---\n\n## N01 CHAPTER LOGGING (CURRENT STATUS)\n\n### **Locked Chapters (1-5):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 1 | Something Wrong | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 2 | Glass That Listens | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 3 | The Sky Breaks | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 4 | After the Shudder | BEAT_02 | Beat 1 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n| 5 | Lines That Don't Lie | BEAT_01 | Beat 10 | 🔒 LOCKED |\n\n**Note:** Ch 1-2 use BEAT_01 consecutively (opening exception for parallel POV establishment). Ch 5 returns to BEAT_01 for systematic observation/pattern work.\n\n---\n\n### **Remaining Chapters (6-12):**\n\n| Chapter | Title | N01 Local Beat | Saga Beat | Status |\n|---------|-------|----------------|-----------|--------|\n| 6 | Weight That Won't Yield | BEAT_03 | Beat 3 | To Write |\n| 7 | Quiet Calculations | BEAT_08 | Beat 21 | To Write |\n| 8 | Proof and Resis\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765796950.9294243,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "6bb0bc7d-d918-4dda-872c-d743d87493d8",

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **INTERLUDE II: \"Mobel - Purity Rising\"**\n- **POV:** Mobel (Purity god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self)\n- **Tide:** ~15-16\n- **Setting:** Entry-side (opposite hemisphere from Saint Merro)\n- **Type:** Build-Up\n- **Word Count Target:** 600-900 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Elements:**\n- Sensory overwhelm (pain as purification)\n- Purity Principle bias (absolute judgment)\n- First inter-god awareness\n- Innocent frame (no recognition of divinity)\n- Foreshadows ideological warfare\n\n**Purpose:** Establish second divine force, show Principles in conflict, set up multi-front pressure on protagonists\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },

    "72295fa3-8a9b-492e-be04-d21ea63bb4f2": {
      "children": [
        "161853b5-ef77-4a59-8ea8-67b63b4b0973"
      ],
      "id": "72295fa3-8a9b-492e-be04-d21ea63bb4f2",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "intensity={}\nwordcount={}\ntide={}\nbeat_local={}\nbeat_saga={}\npov={}\nfor ch in range(1,13):\n    txt=sections[f\"CH{ch:02d}\"]\n    # intensity\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Emotional Intensity:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    intensity[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Word Count:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    if not m:\n        m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Target Word Count:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    wordcount[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Tide:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    tide[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Beat Method:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    beat_local[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*Saga Beat:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    beat_saga[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*POV:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    pov[ch]=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\nintensity, wordcount[4], tide[5], beat_local[9]"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797199.585669,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "72295fa3-8a9b-492e-be04-d21ea63bb4f2",

    "74e6a595-764d-41fd-aba5-1afd2b4f4f9b": {
      "children": [
        "1eb2c08d-d70d-43d2-9905-fdd4368e47a9"
      ],
      "id": "74e6a595-764d-41fd-aba5-1afd2b4f4f9b",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## 9. Tiffani Scene Template\n\nFor most Tiffani scenes, you can loosely follow this pattern:\n\n1. **Instrument / environment check**\n   * She runs through tools or conditions in a defined order.\n   * Establish baseline: what \"normal\" looks like.\n\n2. **Initial anomaly**\n   * Small deviation in measurement.\n   * She notes it, records it, labels it (tentative, minor, within range).\n\n3. **Pattern recognition**\n   * She compares to previous Tides or entries.\n   * \"Pattern appears persistent\" moment.\n\n4. **Social friction**\n   * Someone interrupts: guild messenger, council voice, Pella.\n   * Conflict between \"urgent visible tasks\" and her \"invisible risk\" concerns.\n\n5. **Escalation of anomaly**\n   * Deviation increases; new signals appear (glass vibration, taste, visual changes).\n   * She re-tests, re-validates instrument, rules out her own error.\n\n6. **Trauma echo / methodological vow**\n   * Brief, concrete reference to Marisse and what she misclassified.\n   * Recommitment: will not call this noise.\n\n7. **Decision**\n   * Document the state.\n   * Choose who to tell or where to go next.\n   * Small physical exit beat (moving toward hatch, stair, council, etc.)\n\n---\n\n## 10. Quick \"Voice Check\" Phrases\n\nIf a passage feels off, check against these example lines from Chapter 2:\n\n**Analysis & Self-Correction:**\n* \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n* \"Persistent meant repeatable. Repeatable meant real. Real meant it could hurt someone.\"\n* \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n**Marisse Trauma (Methodological):**\n* \"Noise killed Mariss\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860350.7802286,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "74e6a595-764d-41fd-aba5-1afd2b4f4f9b",

            "code": "# print section 9\ns9 = tiff.find(\"## 9.\")\nprint(tiff[s9:s9+1600])\n",
            "end_time": 1765860351.0800648,

                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "## 9. Quick \"Voice Check\" Phrases\n\nIf a passage feels off, check against these example lines from Chapter 2:\n\n**Analysis & Self-Correction:**\n* \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n* \"Persistent meant repeatable.                 "text": "## 9. Tiffani Scene Template\n\nFor most Tiffani scenes, you can loosely follow this pattern:\n\n1. **Instrument / environment check**\n   * She runs through tools or conditions in a defined order.\n   * Establish baseline: what \"normal\" looks like.\n\n2. **Initial anomaly**\n   * Small deviation in measurement.\n   * She notes it, records it, labels it (tentative, minor, within range).\n\n3. **Pattern recognition**\n   * She compares to previous Tides or entries.\n   * \"Pattern appears persistent\" moment.\n\n4. **Social friction**\n   * Someone interrupts: guild messenger, council voice, Pella.\n   * Conflict between \"urgent visible tasks\" and her \"invisible risk\" concerns.\n\n5. **Escalation of anomaly**\n   * Deviation increases; new signals appear (glass vibration, taste, visual changes).\n   * She re-tests, re-validates instrument, rules out her own error.\n\n6. **Trauma echo / methodological vow**\n   * Brief, concrete reference to Marisse and what she misclassified.\n   * Recommitment: will not call this noise.\n\n7. **Decision**\n   * Document the state.\n   * Choose who to tell or where to go next.\n   * Small physical exit beat (moving toward hatch, stair, council, etc.)\n\n---\n\n## 10. Quick \"Voice Check\" Phrases\n\nIf a passage feels off, check against these example lines from Chapter 2:\n\n**Analysis & Self-Correction:**\n* \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n* \"Persistent meant repeatable.     "74ff6b96-da03-4951-93ff-d2c79b43e4f6": {
      "children": [
        "a7c733d4-fcdb-42a6-a392-dd764f1fa4b0"
      ],
      "id": "74ff6b96-da03-4951-93ff-d2c79b43e4f6",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "ning)\n\n**Turning Metaphors:**\n- Turning 1 (N01-N05): **GLASS** - Fragile emergence, first formation, transparent but breakable\n- Turning 2 (N06-N10): **CRACK** - Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n- Turning 3 (N11-N15): **FORGE** - Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n- Turning 4 (N16-N20): **SMELT** - Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n- Turning 5 (N21-N25): **CLEAR** - Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- Turning 6 (N26-N30): **BREAK** - Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Each Turning represents approximately 1 year of story time.**\n\n### 1.2 NOVELLA STRUCTURAL LAWS\n\n**EVERY novella must contain:**\n\n**15 Numbered Chapters**\n- Word count per chapter: 800-1,300 words (NEVER exceed)\n- Total novella word count: ~18,000-21,000 words\n- **EXCEPTION:** N04-N05 have 17 chapters for Turning 1 escalation (20,000-24,000 words)\n- **N06+ returns to 15-chapter standard**\n\n- Placement: Between Chapters 4-13 (mid-novella interruptions)\n- Minimum separation: 2 chapters apart\n- Must vary placement b\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797021.999693,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "74ff6b96-da03-4951-93ff-d2c79b43e4f6",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "print(meta[2100:3200])",
            "end_time": 1765797022.0818467,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [
              {
                "content": {
                  "execution_state": "busy"
                },
                "msg_type": "status",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "a1cd7b8a-218e1614ff485bd02776d0f0_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"
                }

              {
                "msg_type": "execute_input",
                "parent_header": {

                  "version": "5.3"

              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "ning)\n\n**Turning Metaphors:**\n- Turning 1 (N01-N05): **GLASS** - Fragile emergence, first formation, transparent but breakable\n- Turning 2 (N06-N10): **CRACK** - Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n- Turning 3 (N11-N15): **FORGE** - Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n- Turning 4 (N16-N20): **SMELT** - Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n- Turning 5 (N21-N25): **CLEAR** - Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- Turning 6 (N26-N30): **BREAK** - Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Each Turning represents approximately 1 year of story time.**\n\n### 1.2 NOVELLA STRUCTURAL LAWS\n\n**EVERY novella must contain:**\n\n**15 Numbered Chapters**\n- Word count per chapter: 800-1,300 words (NEVER exceed)\n- Total novella word count: ~18,000-21,000 words\n- **EXCEPTION:** N04-N05 have 17 chapters for Turning 1 escalation (20,000-24,000 words)\n- **N06+ returns to 15-chapter standard**\n\n- Placement: Between Chapters 4-13 (mid-novella interruptions)\n- Minimum separation: 2 chapters apart\n- Must vary placement b\n"
                },
                "msg_type": "stream",
                "parent_header": {
                  "msg_id": "a1cd7b8a-218e1614ff485bd02776d0f0_3_1",
                  "version": "5.3"

                }

            "messages": [

                "sender": "server",

                "text": "ning)\n\n**Turning Metaphors:**\n- Turning 1 (N01-N05): **GLASS** - Fragile emergence, first formation, transparent but breakable\n- Turning 2 (N06-N10): **CRACK** - Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n- Turning 3 (N11-N15): **FORGE** - Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n- Turning 4 (N16-N20): **SMELT** - Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n- Turning 5 (N21-N25): **CLEAR** - Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- Turning 6 (N26-N30): **BREAK** - Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Each Turning represents approximately 1 year of story time.**\n\n### 1.2 NOVELLA STRUCTURAL LAWS\n\n**EVERY novella must contain:**\n\n**15 Numbered Chapters**\n- Word count per chapter: 800-1,300 words (NEVER exceed)\n- Total novella word count: ~18,000-21,000 words\n- **EXCEPTION:** N04-N05 have 17 chapters for Turning 1 escalation (20,000-24,000 words)\n- **N06+ returns to 15-chapter standard**\n\n- Placement: Between Chapters 4-13 (mid-novella interruptions)\n- Minimum separation: 2 chapters apart\n- Must vary placement b\n",

    "777aa7fa-9b4f-4313-8d70-19f47f67806c": {

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": " her will\n\n**Church/Guild/Authority:**\n- Confused and reactive\n- Theorize it's contamination, possession, or heresy\n- Begin registration/suppression attempts (fail)\n- No framework for understanding yet\n\n### What Gets Revealed in T1\n\n**On-Page (Characters Learn):**\n- N01: Soulpulse can stabilize and break structures\n- N02: Some people are naturally aligned to it; others struggle or can't access\n- N03: It has costs; use causes fatigue, tremors, pain\n- N04: Cost escalates with intensity; there are hard limits\n- N05: One person can hold multiple Principles briefly (Jhace's Fathom Break is proof)\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797012.8050194,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "777aa7fa-9b4f-4313-8d70-19f47f67806c",
