                  "text": " her will\n\n**Church/Guild/Authority:**\n- Confused and reactive\n- Theorize it's contamination, possession, or heresy\n- Begin registration/suppression attempts (fail)\n- No framework for understanding yet\n\n### What Gets Revealed in T1\n\n**On-Page (Characters Learn):**\n- N01: Soulpulse can stabilize and break structures\n- N02: Some people are naturally aligned to it; others struggle or can't access\n- N03: It has costs; use causes fatigue, tremors, pain\n- N04: Cost escalates with intensity; there are hard limits\n- N05: One person can hold multiple Principles briefly (Jhace's Fathom Break is proof)\n\n\n"

          "text": "# Split by chapter headings\nsections={}\npattern=r'### \\*\\*CHAPTER (\\d+): \"([^\"]+)\"\\*\\*'\nmatches=list(re.finditer(pattern, grid))\nfor i,m in enumerate(matches):\n    start=m.start()\n    end=matches[i+1].start() if i+1<len(matches) else len(grid)\n    chapnum=int(m.group(1))\n    title=m.group(2)\n    sections[f\"CH{chapnum:02d}\"]=grid[start:end]\nsections[\"CH01\"][:1200]"
        },
        "create_time": 1765796976.704055,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "78fdf8fe-7085-459d-85ef-37a37c605058",

              "content": "There's a conflict between the beat catalog and the locked Bible. The catalog says you can't immediately follow another BEAT_01, but the Bible includes both Ch1 and Ch2 with BEAT_01. It might be that the catalog is general, allowing for exceptions in specific cases like the locked Bible. I'll need to investigate if “immediately following” refers to the same POV.",

    "7aee585b-16f5-41a8-b338-25f7f49761e9": {
      "children": [
        "9445ec7f-2640-40a8-be0e-46afd0f964c6"
      ],
      "id": "7aee585b-16f5-41a8-b338-25f7f49761e9",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": "final",
        "content": {
          "content_type": "text",
          "parts": [
            "## CHAPTER 1 — Something Wrong  \n*Jhace POV | Tide 0 | Kindling*\n\nJhace’s boots found the same three dents in the stone at the lip of the run, and he stopped there anyway. Habit did not excuse speed. The Underface was never steady. It only pretended.\n\nHeat rose from the main lines in a constant push, the kind that dried sweat into salt and made metal feel soft. The arch above him carried the city’s weight in clean curves and old repairs, each brace married to stone with pins and wedges that had held through more Tides than most of the men who walked beneath them.\n\nHe set his palm to the nearest pipe.\n\nThe thrum met his skin through leather and callus. One. Two. Three. A steady cadence he had learned before he had learned to read. He counted without moving his mouth. He let the vibration settle into his wrist, into his elbow, into the bone.\n\nEven. Warm. Carrying.\n\nHe moved on.\n\nJhace worked alone when he could. Too many boots made too much noise, and noise hid the small warnings. Bram said that was just Jhace’s excuse for being difficult. Bram also said difficult kept people alive, then grinned so it sounded harmless.\n\nThis run belonged to the Saint Merro district, three levels under the market spill and one level above the drainage. The air tasted of metal and old grit. Far off, a drip counted its own slow rhythm into a shallow catch. The Underface had always had a thousand small sounds. Jhace listened for the ones that changed.\n\nHe ran his fingers along a bracket at shoulder height. The iron was slick with heat, but the edges were clean. No burrs. No fresh scrape from shift.\n\nHe crouched and checked the stone beneath the bracket’s foot. The seam line held tight. No powdering. No damp line.\n\n“Enough,” he muttered, not to comfort himself, but to name the check complete.\n\nHe stood and walked deeper, following the main toward the first marry point. His hand stayed near the wall so he could steady himself if the floor bucked. It had bucked twice in the last set of Tides. Small tremors. Normal in the Crown’s bones since the old quakes. Normal did not mean safe.\n\nAt the second brace, he paused again. The brace took the load from an overhead arch and bled it into a column that ran down into unseen stone. Jhace pressed his knuckles to the column and listened through touch.\n\nThe vibration felt thinner.\n\nNot weaker. Thinner. The pulse had the same count, but the contact was wrong. The column carried it without depth.\n\nHe pulled his hand away and pressed it back. Same. He shifted his palm higher, then lower, then to the brace itself.\n\nThe brace carried full.\n\nThe column did not.\n\nJhace took a piece of chalk from his belt pouch and drew a small mark on the stone, simple and sharp. He did not write a warning for anyone else. The Underface signs were for those who knew how to read them, and the ones who did not would ignore words anyway.\n\nHe made himself keep moving.\n\nThe next run split. One path led toward the Glass Quarter supports. The other led toward the harbor spill and the older stones under the Crown.\n\nJhace chose the harbor side. He did not trust the older stones today.\n\nThat thought arrived without a reason he could point to. He did not like thoughts without reasons.\n\nHe stopped at the split and took the wall in both hands. Cool stone under warm air. Rough grit under his fingertips. He counted pulses through the surface.\n\nOne. Two. Three.\n\nA pause that should not have been there.\n\nHe counted again. The pause repeated, not exact, but close enough to call it consistent.\n\nHe shifted his ear to the stone. The sound was not a clean thrum. It carried a faint rasp in its tail, a texture that belonged to stressed metal or shifting grit.\n\nHe straightened and looked down the harbor run. The lamps were spaced by rule, but the light seemed to end sooner than it should. The far end sat in a deeper dark.\n\nHe blinked once. The lamps did not change. His eyes were fine.\n\nThe dark remained too deep.\n\nHe moved into it anyway.\n\nThe air changed after four paces. The heat from the main lines dropped, not fully, but enough that his sweat cooled on his back. A cold band in the tunnel, shallow and clean, running straight across the run.\n\nJhace stopped at its edge and held his palm out into it.\n\nThe cold did not bite his skin. It was not that kind of cold. It was a lack of heat, deliberate in its boundary. He withdrew his hand and checked the pipe beside him.\n\nThe pipe was still warm.\n\nHe pressed his palm to the pipe and held it there. The vibration came through, but the count felt slightly off. Not the beat itself. The way it carried between beats. The silence was too tight.\n\nHe moved his hand along the pipe. Warm. Warm. Warm. Then the warmth dipped for a span, then returned.\n\nA heat line does not dip for a span without a cause.\n\nJhace crouched and checked for a seep. No damp. No mineral line. No hiss.\n\nHe rose and checked the bracket pins. They were seated. No fresh shift marks. He put his weight into the brace and felt for give. The brace did not move.\n\nHe checked the stone around it with a light tap from his knuckles. Solid. No hollow note.\n\nHe took his chalk and marked the boundary of the cold band on the wall, two short lines and a cross. His hand moved fast now, not sloppy, but urgent.\n\nHe had planned to finish this run before Kindling ended. Then he would return to the forge, take food, take heat that belonged to him rather than the grid, and sleep for a full rest block. Bram had promised stew. Bram always promised stew and then made it happen through stubbornness alone.\n\nJhace looked at the chalk lines.\n\nHe did not turn back.\n\nHe continued toward the marry point.\n\nThe harbor run widened there, stone arched higher to carry the weight of a surface tower. That tower held a water tank, a load that never stopped pressing down. Jhace had checked the marry point twice each Tide since the last tremor. It had held. It had always held.\n\nHe reached it and immediately knew it was wrong.\n\nThe sound was not one sound. It was two, laid over each other without agreement. The main line’s steady thrum and a second pulse, faint but persistent, coming up through the floor.\n\nJhace set his boot heel on the stone and shifted weight. The floor answered with a subtle give that did not exist in this section. Not enough to see, but enough to feel. The stone had a softness at its edge.\n\nHe knelt and pressed his palm flat to the floor.\n\nThe second pulse traveled up his arm.\n\nIt did not match the grid’s cadence. It ran between beats, as if the stone itself had learned a new rhythm.\n\nHe lifted his hand, then pressed it again. Same.\n\nHe moved a span to the left and tried again. Same, but stronger.\n\nHe moved a span to the right. Weaker.\n\nA source. A gradient.\n\nJhace’s mouth went dry. He swallowed once, tasting grit.\n\nHe should send word.\n\nHe should shut down a section and clear the run.\n\nHe should not do those things without proof. The Underface supervisors did not close spans of tunnel on a feeling. They wanted a seam crack with daylight in it. They wanted a bracket half-sheared. They wanted bodies before they wanted paperwork.\n\nJhace sat back on his heels. He looked at the marry point’s metal sleeve where two runs joined. The sleeve was bolted into stone, old work. Good work.\n\nHe placed his fingers against the sleeve and felt its vibration.\n\nSteady. Then the faint second pulse rode beneath it, making the steady thrum feel slightly wrong by contact alone.\n\nJhace pulled his hand away and stared at his own fingers. They shook once, a small tremor he could blame on heat and fatigue.\n\nHe had not eaten since the last rest block. Heat pulled hunger out of the body. It also pulled judgment, if you let it.\n\nHe stood and forced his breathing slow.\n\nNo panic. Only checks.\n\nHe took the edge of his chalk and scraped a small square on the sleeve, then drew a line through it. A mark for “recheck” and “do not ignore.” He drew another mark on the floor where the second pulse felt strongest.\n\nThen he followed the gradient.\n\nThe stone under his boots changed with each pace. Not loose, not cracking, but subtly more alive in its vibration. The cold band returned under his skin again, but now it stretched farther along the run, not a clean boundary. It feathered. It spread.\n\nJhace stopped and put his palm to the wall.\n\nThe wall’s vibration carried the second pulse in full now. He could count it. He could name it. It repeated every five beats of the main, then slipped, then returned again, consistent enough to be a pattern and inconsistent enough to resist comfort.\n\nHe lowered his ear to the stone. The rasp in the tail grew clearer. Fine grit moving where it should be still.\n\nHis mind offered up a list without asking.\n\nLoose fill. Drain shift. Water undermining. Pin failure. Hidden crack.\n\nHe rejected each one in turn. No damp. No smell of fresh break. No hollow sound. No sudden heat loss that matched a leak.\n\nThe pattern did not fit the old causes.\n\nJhace straightened. He did not like not fitting.\n\nHe moved faster, boots careful on the stone, body angled so he could catch himself if the floor decided to drop.\n\nHe passed another split and took the lower run, heading toward a support section that should have been stable by design. Three arches in a row, redundant bracing, a place the Underface called safe because it had been safe.\n\nThe air down here carried less heat, more wet stone. The lamps hissed softly. His own breath sounded too loud.\n\nAt the first arch, he checked the left brace.\n\nThe brace was tight.\n\nHe checked the right brace.\n\nTight.\n\nHe checked the column.\n\nThinner again. That same lack of depth, that same sense that the vibration was traveling without its usual weight behind it.\n\nHe moved to the second arch. Same.\n\nThird arch. Same.\n\nThree in a row.\n\nThat was not a local fault. That was spread.\n\nJhace took out his small slate from his belt and held it against his thigh. He wrote with chalk, short strokes, no extra words.\n\n**Harbor run: cold band.**  \n**Marry point: second pulse. Gradient.**  \n**Lower arches: thin carry. Spread.**\n\nHe stared at the slate.\n\nHe thought of a different slate, old and stained, from a year when he had been smaller and less capable. A building had come down in a loud clean failure, and the count afterward had been twenty-three bodies. Two had shared his family name. Two Torrins pulled from dust and stone, their faces wrong, their hands still.\n\nHe did not let himself go deeper into that memory. He did not need to. The count lived in him without permission.\n\nHe tucked the slate away and began to backtrack toward the last split. He moved fast but did not run. Running in the Underface made you miss a weak grate. It made you die for speed.\n\nAs he approached the split, he heard voices ahead. Boots. Two men, maybe three, talking loud to make the tunnel feel less empty.\n\nBram’s laugh carried first, sharp and familiar, cutting through the Underface’s dull noise.\n\nJhace stopped and waited until Bram rounded the corner with a lantern hooked to his belt and a bundle of cloth under one arm. Bram’s hair was damp from heat and his cheeks carried soot. He looked well-fed. He looked offended by Jhace’s existence.\n\n“There you are,” Bram said. “I had to ask three runners to find you. All of them said you were where you should not be.”\n\nJhace glanced past him. Two other grid hands stood back, watching the walls more than the conversation. Bram had brought witnesses. Bram did not do anything without thinking.\n\nJhace said, “I am working.”\n\nBram snorted. “You are always working. Kindling ends and you keep working. Pouring comes and you keep working. You will work through Quench and call it normal.”\n\nJhace did not answer that. It was too close to true to waste words on.\n\nBram held up the cloth bundle. “Stew. Still warm. You will eat it before you pretend the tunnel has become your bed.”\n\nJhace looked at the stew and felt nothing at first. Hunger had been pushed down so far it had become a distant pressure rather than a call. He took the bundle anyway because Bram would not let it go.\n\nHe said, “Something is wrong.”\n\nBram’s expression changed. The grin dropped. His eyes sharpened. Bram could joke through most things. When Jhace used those words, Bram stopped.\n\n“What,” Bram said. Not a question in tone. An inventory request.\n\nJhace pointed back down the run. “Harbor side. A cold band where heat should be even. A second pulse at the marry point. Spread thin carry in lower arches.”\n\nOne of the grid hands behind Bram shifted his lantern higher, shining it on the wall. His eyes moved over Jhace’s chalk marks.\n\nBram said, “Did you shut anything down?”\n\n“No,” Jhace said. “Not yet.”\n\nBram exhaled once through his nose. “Not yet means you are choosing to find proof.”\n\nJhace kept his face still. He kept his voice flat. “Yes.”\n\nBram looked at the other men. “You heard him. We do not split. We do not wander. We check the marry point and the lower arches. We log it clean. Then we decide.”\n\nOne of the men said, “This is a lot to close for a feeling.”\n\nJhace’s head turned toward him. “It is not a feeling.”\n\nHe stepped closer to the man and put his palm on the wall beside the man’s shoulder. He did not touch the man. He touched the stone.\n\n“Put your hand here,” Jhace said.\n\nThe man hesitated. Bram did not speak. Bram let the Underface teach.\n\nThe man placed his palm on the wall.\n\nHis face changed in a slow way. Confusion first. Then attention.\n\n“That,” the man said, and stopped.\n\nJhace nodded once. “That.”\n\nBram’s jaw tightened. “Eat,” Bram said to Jhace, because Bram believed in sequences. “Then show us the gradient.”\n\nJhace unwrapped the cloth bundle and took one mouthful of stew. Salt. Fat. Heat. His stomach tightened around it, surprised. He swallowed and took another. Hunger woke in a rush, angry at being ignored.\n\nHe ate enough to stop the hollow feeling. Not enough to slow him down.\n\nHe rewrapped the bundle and handed it back to Bram. Bram took it without argument. Bram knew when Jhace would not accept more.\n\nJhace led them back toward the marry point.\n\nAs they walked, Jhace kept his hand near the wall again. The second pulse was clearer now, not because it grew, but because his mind had named it. Once named, it would not hide. It ran under everything, a persistent wrongness in the Underface’s bones.\n\nHis boots crossed the boundary of the cold band, and his skin prickled under his shirt. Bram felt it too; Bram’s shoulders rose slightly, then settled. Bram did not comment. Bram saved words for decisions.\n\nThey reached the marry point. Jhace watched Bram go down to one knee and put his palm flat to the floor.\n\nBram’s eyes narrowed. He counted under his breath, lips barely moving.\n\nOne. Two. Three.\n\nThen Bram’s count faltered. He lifted his hand and pressed it down again, hard enough to grind grit into skin.\n\nHe looked up at Jhace. The humor was gone entirely now. Bram did not ask for more proof. Bram had felt it.\n\nBram said, “We log. We send a runner to the Underface seat. We keep people out of the harbor run until someone higher pretends to listen.”\n\nJhace nodded once. He did not trust someone higher to pretend fast enough, but he did not argue with the first step.\n\nBram turned to the other men. “You,” he said, pointing at the taller one. “Run. Fast and clean. Do not tell stories. Tell the marks and the pulse.”\n\nThe man nodded and went.\n\nJhace watched the lantern light recede down the tunnel, then turned back to the stone under his own boots. The second pulse persisted. The pattern held.\n\nThe Underface did not shift. Not yet.\n\nJhace did not relax.\n\nHe pressed his palm to the floor one more time and counted again, committing the count to memory. He wanted the exact wrongness in his head so he could recognize its change.\n\nHe lifted his hand.\n\nAbove them, somewhere beyond stone and brace and pipe, the city carried on with its Kindling noise: carts, voices, work, hunger, pride. None of it reached down here cleanly. Down here, the Crown’s weight mattered more than its songs.\n\nJhace stood.\n\n“Work first,” he said, and began the next check."
          ]
        },

    "7c8e56eb-0cba-401a-a769-2e8dcc37f61d": {
      "children": [
        "c60cb9fb-f8bc-45d8-af5f-345e9e65bc2e"
      ],
      "id": "7c8e56eb-0cba-401a-a769-2e8dcc37f61d",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "file_search",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn1file2 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "## BEAT CATALOG — N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine all beat methods used in N01 so chapters can be:\n\n- Planned and validated structurally.  \n- Kept unpredictable at the series level.  \n- Emotionally coherent within themselves.\n\nN01 uses **eight** beat methods: BEAT_01 through BEAT_08.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Baseline Observation**  \n   - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n   - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n\n2. **Focused Attention**  \n   - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n   - Compares against prior experience.\n\n3. **Testing / Probing**  \n   - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n   - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n\n4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n   - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n   - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n\n5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n   - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nCuriosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Introducing a core problem.  \n- Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n- Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n- Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when you want a **hard failure** that escalates, followed by a victory that hurts.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Trial Setup**  \n   - Goal is defined (save someone, stabilize structure, train Soulpulse).  \n   - Plan seems plausible but not guaranteed.\n\n2. **Initial Attempt → Failure**  \n   - First attempt fails clearly.  \n   - Stakes increase.\n\n3. **Compounding Problem**  \n   - Failure makes situation worse (more damage, less time, more people at risk).\n\n4. **Reframe / New Approach**  \n   - POV changes approach, often with help from another character.\n\n5. **Success with Explicit Cost**  \n   - Goal is achieved or partially achieved.  \n   - Cost is visible: physical, emotional, moral, or relational.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nHope → Frustration → Panic → Determination → Relief + Dread\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Training episodes that go wrong.  \n- Major rescue that almost fails.  \n- Moments where price of success must be undeniable.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot follow another BEAT_02.  \n- Should not directly precede BEAT_03 in most cases (risk of fatigue).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Kylar’s first training attempt + follow-up.  \n- A rescue where Jhace’s first plan fails, and Tiffani’s input salvages it.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_03 — Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for big chaos chapters, especially disasters in progress.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Drop-In Crisis**  \n   - Chapter opens mid-disaster: building already collapsing, Piercing underway, etc.\n\n2. **Rolling Complications**  \n   - Every attempt to stabilize reveals new problems.  \n   - Stakes grow organically (more people, more structure at risk).\n\n3. **Local Focus Choice**  \n   - POV must choose where to focus attention.  \n   - Implicit acceptance that some things will be left to fail.\n\n4. **Partial Resolution**  \n   - Immediate crisis is stabilized enough to survive.  \n   - Larger structural problem remains unsolved.\n\n5. **Fallout Preview**  \n   - Hints at future consequences for this imperfect solution.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nShock → Scramble → Triage → Exhausted Survival → Uneasy Aftermath\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Piercing event itself.  \n- Major structural disaster chapters.  \n- Big pivot moments in the novella.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot be used more than 3 times in N01.  \n- Never back-to-back.  \n- Must be buffered by lower-intensity beats.\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 3 (The Piercing).  \n- Ch 6 (major hold).  \n- Ch 14 (district-spanning crisis).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_04 — Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal or Choice\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for quieter chapters focusing on **decision-making and value lines**.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Recovery / Quiet**  \n   - Start after a high-intensity chapter.  \n   - Characters are physically and/or emotionally worn out.\n\n2. **Reflection**  \n   - POV reflects on what just happened; no info-dump, just internal processing.\n\n3. **Offer or Demand**  \n   - Someone presents a clear, structured option:  \n     - Institutional demand.  \n     - Moral compromise.  \n     - Invitation to join or align.\n\n4. **Evaluation**  \n   - POV weighs what this offer means against their values and constraints.\n\n5. **Line in the Sand**  \n   - POV either refuses, accepts with conditions, or chooses an alternative.  \n   - The choice shapes later conflict.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nExhaustion → Clarity → Temptation/Pressure → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Institutional powers try to co-opt characters.  \n- Community leaders invite alignment.  \n- Characters must decide “who we are” after the chaos.\n\n**Constraints:**\n- Should follow a high-intensity chapter.  \n- Should not be stacked with another BEAT_04; variety required.\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Tiffani deciding to study Soulpulse rather than ignore it.  \n- J/T choosing Salee’s networks over RRO’s priorities.  \n- N01’s closing commitment scene counts as a BEAT_04 variant.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_05 — Training Loop → Conflict → Reframe\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for chapters centered on teaching/learning where methodology matters.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Training Setup**  \n   - Clear objective: “We’re going to teach X to do Y under these conditions.”\n\n2. **Execution → Friction**  \n   - Lesson proceeds.  \n   - Student or situation doesn’t respond as expected.  \n   - Misalignment arises (teacher vs. student, or method vs. reality).\n\n3. **Conflict**  \n   - Argument or tension about what the right approach should be.  \n   - Both sides have partial truth.\n\n4. **Reframe**  \n   - Third factor or self-awareness shifts understanding.  \n   - Training method changes.\n\n5. **Partial Success**  \n   - New approach produces better results but doesn’t perfect the skill.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nHope → Frustration → Clash → Insight → Cautious Optimism\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Teaching Soulpulse control.  \n- Reworking structural response procedures.  \n- Showing philosophical differences in action.\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot follow another BEAT_05.  \n- Should not be adjacent to BEAT_06 (two “talky” beats in a row).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Kylar training sequence (first failure, later success).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_06 — Debate → Third-Party Insight → Synthesis\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for **philosophical or methodological debates** that must resolve into a shared approach.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Defined Disagreement**  \n   - Two characters or factions disagree about how to proceed.  \n   - Each has valid concerns.\n\n2. **Escalation**  \n   - Raised voices, sharper arguments, or entrenched positions.  \n   - Risk that relationship or project breaks.\n\n3. **Third-Party Insight**  \n   - Someone not originally in the debate offers a perspective, or  \n   - One party suddenly reframes the issue based on new understanding.\n\n4. **Synthesis**  \n   - A new, combined approach emerges.  \n   - Not a compromise where both lose; a genuine “third option.”\n\n5. **New Ground Rule**  \n   - Concrete implementation of the new approach (often seen in next chapter).\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nTension → Impasse → Illumination → Agreement\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- J/T conflict over teaching methods.  \n- Institutional vs. community arguments where something new must be born.\n\n**Constraints:**\n- Use very sparingly (1–2 times max in N01).  \n- Never back-to-back with BEAT_05.\n\n**N01 Example (intended):**\n- Asking Kylar what he needs and rebuilding the training approach around that.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_07 — Political Pressure → Moral Line in Sand\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for chapters where **institutional power** tries to force the protagonists into a shape.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Summons / Confrontation**  \n   - Characters are called to, or cornered by, power: RRO, guilds, etc.\n\n2. **Demands Stated**  \n   - Clear requirements:  \n     - Registration.  \n     - Controlled deployment.  \n     - Privileged priority list.\n\n3. **Moral/Internal Conflict**  \n   - POV wrestles with:  \n     - The logic of central control.  \n     - The cost to neglected people.  \n     - Their own desire to help vs. autonomy.\n\n4. **Refusal / Conditional Acceptance**  \n   - They draw a line:  \n     - “We won’t be owned.”  \n     - Or “We’ll help, but not under your terms.”\n\n5. **Immediate Consequence**  \n   - Loss of support, hostility, surveillance, or open enmity.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nPressure → Anger → Doubt → Defiance → Fallout\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Establishing antagonistic relationships with institutions.  \n- Showing that the protagonists’ ethics actively cost them.\n\n**Constraints:**\n- Max twice in N01.  \n- Never used in consecutive chapters.\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- RRO registration confrontation.  \n- J/T choosing Dregs over politically important districts.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_08 — Recovery → Intimacy → Ongoing Commitment\n\n**Function:**  \nUse for capstone chapters where **the relationship and long-term choice** are the focus.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Post-Crisis Quiet**  \n   - Physically and emotionally drained.  \n   - Immediate dangers paused, not eliminated.\n\n2. **Vulnerability**  \n   - Characters admit things they would normally hide:  \n     - Fear.  \n     - Regret.  \n     - Desire.  \n     - Exhaustion.\n\n3. **Intimacy**  \n   - Non-sexual or lightly-sexual closeness:  \n     - Touch, proximity, shared silence.  \n     - Honest talk.\n\n4. **Articulation of “We”**  \n   - They name the partnership for what it is.  \n   - Move from “working together” to “we are choosing this.”\n\n5. **Open-Ended Commitment**  \n   - No neat solution to big world problems.  \n   - Clear commitment to face them together.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nExhaustion → Honesty → Connection → Determination\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- End of first novella arcs.  \n- Major turning point in relationship.\n\n**Constraints:**\n- Only once in N01 (Ch 15).  \n- Must be earned by prior chapters.\n\n**N01 Example (intended):**\n- Jhace and Tiffani’s late-Tide commitment scene.\n\n---\n\n"
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765860054.2592568,
        "end_turn": null,

    "842dd1da-260b-4112-9695-086feee581b0": {

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my choice to call signal noise.\"\n  * \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\nAvoid indulgent grief monologues. Her guilt is real but channeled into stricter process.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Tiffani\n\nWhen Tiffani speaks:\n\n* **More words than Jhace, but still efficient.**\n\n* **Often clarifying or reframing:**\n  * \"It isn't a pastime. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but stay controlled.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy\n\nWe want to keep her technical language consistent, but avoid distracting repetition in tight spans.\n\n### 8.1 Core measurement terms\n\nThese form a small controlled set:\n\n* **noise, signal, pattern, drift, deviation, shift, range, baseline, anomaly, state, source, external, internal, compounding, persistent.**\n\n**Rules:**\n\n* Within a single paragraph, avoid repeating the exact same keyword more than twice unless it's deliberate emphasis (e.\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860337.877204,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "842dd1da-260b-4112-9695-086feee581b0",

                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my choice to call signal noise.\"\n  * \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\nAvoid indulgent grief monologues. Her guilt is real but channeled into stricter process.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Tiffani\n\nWhen Tiffani speaks:\n\n* **More words than Jhace, but still efficient.**\n\n* **Often clarifying or reframing:**\n  * \"It isn't a pastime. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but stay controlled.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy\n\nWe want to keep her technical language consistent, but avoid distracting repetition in tight spans.\n\n### 8.1 Core measurement terms\n\nThese form a small controlled set:\n\n* **noise, signal, pattern, drift, deviation, shift, range, baseline, anomaly, state, source, external, internal, compounding, persistent.**\n\n**Rules:**\n\n* Within a single paragraph, avoid repeating the exact same keyword more than twice unless it's deliberate emphasis (e.\n"

    "87138a88-c596-4d14-ab35-cb1f5e4abbc8": {
      "children": [
        "c4592661-93d1-43ed-9851-31c8b3cf24d3"
      ],
      "id": "87138a88-c596-4d14-ab35-cb1f5e4abbc8",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "7.1 Immediate Physical Cost (Four Stages)\n\nThe short-term cost ladder remains as previously defined, but now explicitly underpins higher-tier forms. [attached_file:10]  \n\n1. **Strain:** Minutes of light use; tremors, headache; recovers in minutes. [attached_file:10]  \n2. **Fatigue:** Extended use; shaking, irritability; recovers overnight. [attached_file:10]  \n3. **Burnout:** Heavy use; collapse risk; recovers over days with care. [attached_file:11]  \n4. **Collapse:** Extreme or Confluent use; unconsciousness; recovers over days to weeks if environment permits. [attached_file:11]  \n\n### 7.2 Long-Term Accrued Cost\n\nRepeated heavy use accumulates **structural wear** on the user: [attached_file:10]  \n\n- Nerve damage and misfires. [attached_file:10]  \n- Chronic pain and joint issues. [attached_file:12]  \n- Emotional blunting or empathy collapse (depending on Principle bias). [attached_file:12]  \n- Life expectancy reduction for Fathombreak-level or repeated high-tier users. [attached_file:11]  \n\n### 7.3 Indirect Fatality\n\nSoulpulse itself does not directly â€œburn outâ€ souls as a mystical execution. [attached_file:10]  \nPeople die because they collapse where buildings are falling, water is rising, or enemies are waiting. [attached_file:7]  \n\n\n---\n\n## 8. RANGE, DURATION, AND VISIBILITY\n\n### 8.1 Range\n\n- Touch: Strongest, most reliable; required for early Turning 1 use. [attached_file:9]  \n- Line-of-sight: Becomes common by late Turning 1 for naturals, more broadly by Turning 2. [attached_file:10]  \n- Through obstacles: Rare mastery achieved mid-saga for a few, requiring intimate knowledge of target structure. [attached_file:14]  \n\nMaximum ranges stay **human-scale** (tens of meters), but effects can propagate further indirectly through structures and systems. [attached_file\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797047.7282114,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "### **CHAPTER 2: \"Glass That Listens\"**\n- **Tide:** 0, Pouring (mid-morning of Piercing)\n- **Setting:** Observatory platform at Saint Merro's rim\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition)\n- **Saga Beat:** Beat 10\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Low\n- **Word Count:** 1,280 words\n- **Status:** 🔒 LOCKED\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Mirrors Ch 1 structure (parallel observation)\n- Pella introduces care/support dynamic\n- Marisse trauma implied through vigilance pattern\n- Atmospheric distortion measurements (first scientific data)\n- Exit as sky splits\n\n**Soulpulse:** Not yet accessible (pre-Piercing)\n\n\n**Stage Progression:** N/A (Stage 0)\n\n---\n\n\n"

    "8a8791b6-f54e-44fe-a4e1-21564461d6d6": {

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "\n=== around Lexicon ===\n\nLexicon & Hard Constraints\n\nThese still apply:\n\n* **No Earth unit systems.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Quench, Turn, span, pace, grain, stone, Weight, etc.\n* **No Earth-specific culture markers.**\n  * No \"sir\" as generic honorific; use setting-appropriate forms (e.g. *Master Jhace*).\n* **No metaphors/similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" to compare things.\n* **Minimal personification.**\n  * Avoid attributing thought/intent to inanimate structures.\n  * **Exception:** Jhace may occasionally frame structures as agents in his internal monologue (e.g., \"the tunnel decided to fail,\" \"the pipe refused to settle\") but this should be rare and clearly his mental shorthand for structural behavior, not narrator voice attributing consciousness.\n* **Te'Oga lexicon for time and work.**\n  * Kindling / Pouring / Shaping / Striking / Quench where appropriate.\n  * Use local work language: brace, marry point, Underface, Glass Quarter, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: What to Zoom In On\n\n### Zoom in \n\n=== around No metaphors ===\n\nNo metaphors/similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" to compare things.\n* **Minimal personification.**\n  * Avoid attributing thought/intent to inanimate structures.\n  * **Exception:** Jhace may occasionally frame structures as agents in his internal monologue (e.g., \"the tunnel decided to fail,\" \"the pipe refused to settle\") but this should be rare and clearly his mental shorthand for structural behavior, not narrator voice attributing consciousness.\n* **Te'Oga lexicon for time and work.**\n  * Kindling / Pouring / Shaping / Striking / Quench where appropriate.\n  * Use local work language: brace, marry point, Underface, Glass Quarter, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: What to Zoom In On\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Jhace is:\n  * Checking a joint, marry point, bracket, or any failure-risk.\n  * Sensing an anomaly in vibration or pattern.\n  * Deciding whether to shut down a line.\n\n**In these moments:**\n\n* Break the action into **micro steps**:\n  * Place palm → count pulses → compare to\n\n=== around Do not ===\n\ndo not close a third of the Underface for a feeling you cannot prove. You do mark it.\"\n\nHe does **not** explain his feelings. He explains **risks** and **outcomes**.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy (Word Rotation)\n\nWe want to avoid distracting repetition without losing Jhace's technical precision.\n\n### 8.1 \"Hum\" and related terms\n\nUse a **small controlled set** and rotate based on nuance:\n\n* **hum** – baseline background sound of the grid.\n* **thrum** – stronger, more insistent pulse, often closer to mains.\n* **vibration** – general physical movement, especially under touch.\n* **resonance** – when structural elements carry and shape the vibration.\n* **pattern** – full combined sense of the tunnel's behavior across senses.\n* **pulse** – discrete beats/units he can count.\n\n**Rule:**\nIn any **short span (1–2 paragraphs)**, avoid repeating the *same* key noun or verb more than twice unless repetition is intentional for emphasis.\n\n### 8.2 Anonymous \"pipe/stone/wall/tunnel\"\n\nRotate wher\n\n=== around Sentence ===\n\nsentence-level* voice for **Jhace POV** in N01, matching the tone, detail, and rhythm of Chapter 1: \"Something Wrong.\"\n\nUse this as the standard whenever writing or revising in Jhace's tight third-person POV.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace's mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   * He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   * Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   * Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his primary channels.\n   * Smell and sight are secondary and mostly used to confirm or refine what he already felt.\n\n3. **Emotion = suppressed background process.**\n   * Feelings are implied by behavior, choices, and sensory focus, not named directly.\n   * He will think in terms of \"risk,\" \"failure,\" \"tolerances,\"\n\n=== around Core ===\n\nCore POV: How Jhace Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. The narration only knows what Jhace knows, notices what he would notice, and cares about what he would care about.\n\n**Jhace's mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Structure first.**\n   * He reads the world as load paths, supports, failure points.\n   * Everything filters through stability, risk, and maintenance.\n\n2. **Sensation as data.**\n   * Touch, vibration, pressure, weight, temperature, and sound are his primary channels.\n   * Smell and sight are secondary and mostly used to confirm or refine what he already felt.\n\n3. **Emotion = suppressed background process.**\n   * Feelings are implied by behavior, choices, and sensory focus, not named directly.\n   * He will think in terms of \"risk,\" \"failure,\" \"tolerances,\" not \"fear\" or \"sadness.\"\n\n4. **History in the hands, not in exposition.**\n   * Past events appear as *embedded associations*: \"This bracket had given him trouble over the last few Tides,\" not full flashback paragraphs.\n \n\n=== around Sensory ===\n\nsensory focus, not named directly.\n   * He will think in terms of \"risk,\" \"failure,\" \"tolerances,\" not \"fear\" or \"sadness.\"\n\n4. **History in the hands, not in exposition.**\n   * Past events appear as *embedded associations*: \"This bracket had given him trouble over the last few Tides,\" not full flashback paragraphs.\n   * The childhood collapse surfaces as specific details: **count of bodies, building collapse, two Torrins**, not vague trauma monologues.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nPlain, concrete, workmanlike. No ornament. Rhythm varies between short, clipped sentences and slightly longer ones that stack observations in sequence.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default:** Short to medium length.\n  * 7–18 words is typical.\n* **Use longer sentences** only to:\n  * Stack **sequential checks** (touch metal → check stone → listen).\n  * Walk through a **decision with consequences**.\n* **Avoid:** Long, looping, lyrical sentences that wander from the physical moment.\n\n#\n"
        },

    "8abf8c43-b7fa-4cbd-9058-450b41f6aee8": {

      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "---- 584\n â€” gods and masks\n- `04-Cosmology-Confluence.md` â€” hidden truth\n- `00-Saga-Blueprint.md` â€” timeline and structure\n\n---\n\n## 1. KNOWLEDGE HIERARCHY\n\n### Universal Baseline (Everyone)\n\nBy start of N01, all Te'Oga citizens know:\n- A massive object pierced the planet\n- Earthquakes and tsunamis resulted\n- Axis tilted; seasons are gone\n- Something called \"Soulpulse\" or \"resonance\" is appearing in some people\n- It seems dangerous and unpredictable\n\n### Doctrine Baseline (Regional Variation)\n\nEvery region has different, often contradictory scripture about:\n- Whether gods once walked Te'Oga or are purely metaphor\n- Which Principle is holy/damned\n\n---- 1624\nal history, old pacts, and how Soulpulse shifts power\n- **Tier 5: Gods Awakening**: Remember fragments of divine nature as seals crack\n\n---\n\n## 2. TURNING 1: GLASS (N01â€“N05)\n\n### Knowledge State at N01 Start\n\n**Jhace:**\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in materials\n- Knows nothing about divine origins or cosmic truth\n- Discovers cost of use through trial and pain\n- Believes Soulpulse is \"new magic from the Piercing debris\"\n\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in glass and structure\n- Same blank slate as Jhace regarding bigger picture\n- By N04: realizes this power has principles/rules, not random\n- By N05: pushes into know\n---- 3367\neorize it's contamination, possession, or heresy\n- Begin registration/suppression attempts (fail)\n- No framework for understanding yet\n\n### What Gets Revealed in T1\n\n**On-Page (Characters Learn):**\n- N01: Soulpulse can stabilize and break structures\n- N02: Some people are naturally aligned to it; others struggle or can't access\n- N03: It has costs; use causes fatigue, tremors, pain\n- N04: Cost escalates with intensity; there are hard limits\n- N05: One person can hold multiple Principles briefly (Jhace's Fathom Break is proof)\n\n- The Piercing was a designed weapon, not random impact\n- Corlexi/Mobel/Salee/Eries are masked gods\n- Gods are beginn\n---- 14113\nimer cycles\n- Whether she will try again after N30\n\n### Character Discovery Moments (On-Page)\n\n| Moment | Turning | Significance |\n|--------|---------|---|\n| Jhace first uses Soulpulse deliberately | N01 | \"This is real and it hurts\" |\n| Involuntary Tier 3 manifestations | N06 | Soulpulse is now universal |\n| Corlexi/Mobel clash openly | N12 | Gods are warring in public |\n| Jhace suspects divine nature | N13â€“N14 | Personal framework shift |\n| Four reveal themselves as gods | N17 | Doctrine becomes explicit |\n| Lattice prevents faction victory | N24 | Understanding that balance = survival |\n| Jhace prevents second Fathom Break | N29 | Endgam\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797007.3182075,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "8abf8c43-b7fa-4cbd-9058-450b41f6aee8",

TURNING 1: GLASS (N01â€“N05)\n\n### Knowledge State at N01 Start\n\n**Jhace:**\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in materials\n- Knows nothing about divine origins or cosmic truth\n- Discovers cost of use through trial and pain\n- Believes Soulpulse is \"new magic from the Piercing debris\"\n\n- Craft-mind: instinctively feels Soulpulse in glass and structure\n- Same blank slate as Jhace regarding bigger picture\n- By N04: realizes this power has principles/rules, not random\n- By N05: pushes into know\n---- 3367\neorize it's contamination, possession, or heresy\n- Begin registration/suppression attempts (fail)\n- No framework for understanding yet\n\n### What Gets Revealed in T1\n\n**On-Page (Characters Learn):**\n- N01: Soulpulse can stabilize and break structures\n- N02: Some people are naturally aligned to it; others struggle or can't access\n- N03: It has costs; use causes fatigue, tremors, pain\n- N04: Cost escalates with intensity; there are hard limits\n- N05: One person can hold multiple Principles briefly (Jhace's Fathom Break is proof)\n\n- The Piercing was a designed weapon, not random impact\n- Corlexi/Mobel/Salee/Eries are masked gods\n- Gods are beginn\n---- 14113\nimer cycles\n- Whether she will try again after N30\n\n### Character Discovery Moments (On-Page)\n\n| Moment | Turning | Significance |\n|--------|---------|---|\n| Jhace first uses Soulpulse deliberately | N01 | \"This is real and it hurts\" |\n| Involuntary Tier 3 manifestations | N06 | Soulpulse is now universal |\n| Corlexi/Mobel clash openly | N12 | Gods are warring in public |\n| Jhace suspects divine nature | N13â€“N14 | Personal framework shift |\n| Four reveal themselves as gods | N17 | Doctrine becomes explicit |\n| Lattice prevents faction victory | N24 | Understanding that balance = survival |\n| Jhace prevents second Fathom Break | N29 | Endgam\n"

          "text": "# SOULPULSE: MAGIC SYSTEM CORE\n**Resonance, Power Stages, Costs, and Limits**\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis document defines how Soulpulse actually works at meta-level, from first emergence after the Piercing through post-Fathom-Break escalation to late-saga combat resonance. [attached_file:9]  \nIt exists to keep power levels, costs, and capabilities consistent across all 30 novellas, even when characters only know fragments in-story. [attached_file:7]  \n\n**Status:** OMNISCIENT CANON (behind-the-scenes truth)  \n**Characters:** Never see this document; they discover partial truths only. [attached_file:9]  \n\nCross-refs:  \n- `00-Saga-Blueprint.md` â€” structure, Turnings, acrostic. [attached_file:6]  \n- `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md` â€” why Soulpulse becomes possible. [attached_file:8]  \n- `03-Divine-Principles.md` â€” gods behind the Principles. [attached_file:12]  \n- `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md` â€” who learns what, when. [attached_file:6]  \n\n---\n\n## 1. ONTOLOGY: WHAT SOULPULSE IS\n\n### 1.1 Friction Engine of Existence\n\nAt cosmic level, existence is driven by constant contradiction between four Divine Principles: Form, Wholeness, Purity, and Entropy. [attached_file:12]  \nThe\n"

              {
                "content": {
                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "# SOULPULSE: MAGIC SYSTEM CORE\n**Resonance, Power Stages, Costs, and Limits**\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis document defines how Soulpulse actually works at meta-level, from first emergence after the Piercing through post-Fathom-Break escalation to late-saga combat resonance. [attached_file:9]  \nIt exists to keep power levels, costs, and capabilities consistent across all 30 novellas, even when characters only know fragments in-story. [attached_file:7]  \n\n**Status:** OMNISCIENT CANON (behind-the-scenes truth)  \n**Characters:** Never see this document; they discover partial truths only. [attached_file:9]  \n\nCross-refs:  \n- `00-Saga-Blueprint.md` â€” structure, Turnings, acrostic. [attached_file:6]  \n- `01-TeOga-Physical-Piercing.md` â€” why Soulpulse becomes possible. [attached_file:8]  \n- `03-Divine-Principles.md` â€” gods behind the Principles. [attached_file:12]  \n- `05-Knowledge-Gates-Discovery.md` â€” who learns what, when. [attached_file:6]  \n\n---\n\n## 1. ONTOLOGY: WHAT SOULPULSE IS\n\n### 1.1 Friction Engine of Existence\n\nAt cosmic level, existence is driven by constant contradiction between four Divine Principles: Form, Wholeness, Purity, and Entropy.                 "text": "# SOULPULSE: MAGIC SYSTEM CORE\n**Resonance, Power Stages, Costs, and Limits**\n\n---\n\n## DOCUMENT PURPOSE\n\nThis document defines how Soulpulse actually works at meta-level, from first emergence after the Piercing through post-Fathom-Break escalation to late-saga combat resonance. [attached_file:9]  \nIt exists to keep power levels, costs, and capabilities consistent across all 30 novellas, even when characters only know fragments in-story.
