    "910bb8ef-ab7c-402e-9b89-534e590006f3": {
      "children": [
        "ce8bb7d2-27c7-4b2e-92b5-de178e80cabf"
      ],
      "id": "910bb8ef-ab7c-402e-9b89-534e590006f3",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "file_search",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn0file0 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "# OmniMeta Saga: Super Exponential Story Bible\n## Complete Narrative Universe Documentation\n\n---\n\n## TABLE OF CONTENTS\n\n1. [Saga Overview](#saga-overview)\n2. [Core Premise](#core-premise)\n3. [Character Profiles](#character-profiles)\n4. [World & Setting](#world--setting)\n5. [Magic System](#magic-system)\n6. [Story Structure](#story-structure)\n7. [Themes & Arcs](#themes--arcs)\n8. [Narrative Rules](#narrative-rules)\n9. [Timeline](#timeline)\n10. [Appendices](#appendices)\n\n---\n\n## SAGA OVERVIEW\n\n**Title:** OmniMeta Saga  \n**Scope:** 30-novella epic fantasy  \n**Structure:** 6 Turnings × 5 novellas each  \n**Acrostic:** GLASS-CRACK-FORGE-SMELT-CLEAR-BREAK  \n**Total Word Count:** ~600,000 words  \n**Timeline:** ~6 years (protagonist ages 28-35)  \n**Current Structure:** 12 chapters + 2 interludes + 1 epilogue per novella  \n\n### The Hidden Message\n\nThe 30 novella titles spell out a dual craft metaphor:\n\n- **GLASS** (Turning 1): Fragile formation, transparent but breakable\n- **CRACK** (Turning 2): Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing  \n- **FORGE** (Turning 3): Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation\n- **SMELT** (Turning 4): Melting down, purification, separation\n- **CLEAR** (Turning 5): Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n- **BREAK** (Turning 6): Final shattering, ultimate transformation\n\n---\n\n## CORE PREMISE\n\n### The Piercing Event\n\nA mysterious projectile pierces completely through the planet Te'Oga, creating a permanent hole and fundamentally breaking planetary physics. This \"Piercing\" event awakens a dormant force called **Soulpulse** that did NOT exist before.\n\n**Physical Consequences:**\n- Axial tilt snapped to ~0° (no meaningful seasons)\n- Catastrophic quakes and tsunamis\n- Permanent climate band disruption\n- \"Scar storms\" near entry/exit wounds\n- Resonance hotspots along damage path\n\n**Metaphysical Consequence:**\n- **Soulpulse/Resonance emerges for the first time**\n- Conscious will can now affect matter\n- Four ancient gods begin awakening in mortal forms\n- The Auditor begins orchestrating events toward stasis\n\n### The Central Conflict\n\n**Jhace Torrins** must evolve from craftsman to leader to god-confronter while navigating:\n- **Partnership with Tiffani** (load-bearing relationship)\n- **Divine awakening** (four gods returning to power)\n- **Auditor's deception** (hidden antagonist orchestrating destruction)\n- **Cost of power** (every use of Soulpulse extracts a physical price)\n\n---\n\n## CHARACTER PROFILES\n\n### Protagonists\n\n#### Jhace Torrins\n- **Age (N01):** 28 turns\n- **Craft:** Forge-smith (gridwright), infrastructure specialist\n- **Psychological Wound:** Survivor's guilt - parents died in structural collapse at age 7, carries 23 names of the dead\n- **Core Drive:** Obsessive need to save everyone, prevent all deaths through vigilance\n- **Soulpulse Alignment:** Primarily FORM (holding, stabilizing, structural integrity)\n- **Character Arc:** Isolated savior → Collaborative leader → God-confronter\n\n**Cost Progression:**\n- Stage 0 (pre-Piercing): No Soulpulse access\n- Stage 1 (N01): Tremor baseline established, vein-lines appearing\n- Approaching Stage 2: Sensory overload warnings\n\n**Key Relationships:**\n- **Tiffani:** Load-bearing partnership - \"remove one, both collapse\"\n- **Bram:** Caretaker figure, provides grounding perspective\n- **Chloen:** Apprentice/protégé, represents legacy\n\n#### Tiffani Merrow\n- **Age (N01):** 26 turns\n- **Craft:** Glassworker, precision measurement and structural analysis\n- **Psychological Wound:** Guilt over friend Marisse's death in glasshouse accident (miscalculation)\n- **Core Drive:** Perfectionism, belief that vigilance alone prevents catastrophe\n- **Soulpulse Alignment:** Primarily WHOLENESS (mending, connecting, restoring)\n- **Ultimate Fate:** Dies in N05 from Soulpulse burnout holding catastrophic structural failure\n- **Character Arc:** Perfectionist isolation → Partnership trust → Sacrificial heroism\n\n**Death Significance:**\n- Triggers Jhace's Fathom Break (supersaiyan moment of grief-powered transcendence)\n- Breaks final seal on four gods (awakens them to ~30% consciousness)\n- Her memory becomes Jhace's driving force for remaining 25 novellas\n\n### The Four God-Vessels\n\n#### Corlexi (Form)\n- **Mortal Name:** Corlexi\n- **Divine Name:** Xilcore\n- **Principle:** FORM - Hold, stabilize, impose structure\n- **Cost Flavor:** Muscle ache, joint stiffness, rigid tension\n- **Danger:** Over-stabilization, inability to adapt, brittleness\n- **Mortal Role:** Administrator, system builder\n- **Divine Nature:** God of structure, order, frameworks\n- **Awakening Stage:** Stage 0 (N01) → progressing to Stage 1\n\n#### Salee (Wholeness)\n- **Mortal Name:** Salee\n- **Divine Name:** Leesa\n- **Principle:** WHOLENESS - Mend, connect, restore continuity\n- **Cost Flavor:** Empathy overload, emotional drain, feeling others' pain\n- **Danger:** Boundary dissolution, losing self in others, compassion paralysis\n- **Mortal Role:** Healer, community builder\n- **Divine Nature:** God of connection, unity, healing\n\n#### Mobel (Purity)\n- **Mortal Name:** Mobel\n- **Divine Name:** Blemo\n- **Principle:** PURITY - Refine, distill, isolate essence\n- **Cost Flavor:** Migraine, hyper-focus burnout, sensory overload\n- **Danger:** Obsessive perfectionism, inability to tolerate imperfection, isolation\n- **Mortal Role:** Judge, purifier\n- **Divine Nature:** God of perfection, separation, righteousness\n\n#### Eries (Entropy)\n- **Mortal Name:** Eries\n- **Divine Name:** Seeri\n- **Principle:** ENTROPY - Undo, release, accelerate decay\n- **Cost Flavor:** Numbness, apathy, delayed collapse, dissociation\n- **Danger:** Nihilism, inability to care, destructive spirals\n- **Mortal Role:** Liberator, collapse artist\n- **Divine Nature:** God of endings, release, natural decay\n\n### The Antagonist\n\n#### The Auditor / Counter-Spark\n- **True Identity:** Counter-Spark (Stasis principle) in mortal form\n- **Narrative Role:** Unreliable narrator across all interludes and epilogues\n- **Voice:** Profane, sardonic, knowing, contemptuous\n- **Facade:** Appears supportive of god awakening, documents events as neutral observer\n- **True Goal:** Shepherding gods toward full power TO ELIMINATE THEM PERMANENTLY\n- **Revelation Timing:** Turning 6 (N26-N30)\n- **Dramatic Irony:** Readers eventually realize every \"observation\" is hostile manipulation\n\n---\n\n## WORLD & SETTING\n\n### Planet Te'Oga\n\n**Physical Reality:**\n- Mysterious projectile has pierced completely through the planet\n- Creates literal hole - gaping, permanent wound\n- Axial tilt snapped to ~0° (essentially upright)\n- No more meaningful seasons - climate zones are static\n- Catastrophic quakes and tsunamis reshaped continents\n\n**Cultural Response:**\n- Guilds control craft knowledge and resource distribution\n- RRO (Resonance Regulation Office) attempts to govern Soulpulse use\n- Factions form around Principle alignments as gods awaken\n- Black market training proliferates outside institutional control\n\n### Time Measurement System\n\n**Absolute Constraint:** Never use Earth time terms (day, week, month, year, etc.)\n\n**Hierarchy:**\n- **Tide** = ~24 hours (one day)\n  - Phases: Kindling (dawn) → Pouring (mid-morning) → Shaping (noon) → Striking (afternoon) → Quench (dusk)\n- **Anneal** = ~7 tides (~1 week)\n- **Crucible** = ~30 tides (~1 month)\n- **Turn** = ~1 year (personal age unit)\n- **Cast** = ~1 year (historical/calendar unit)\n- **Turning** = ~5-10 casts (historical era)\n\n### Measurement Units\n\n**Absolute Constraint:** Never use Earth measurement units (inch, foot, meter, mile, etc.)\n\n**Length:**\n- **Span** = ~1 foot\n- **Stride** = ~1 yard\n- **Reach** = ~10 feet\n- **Throw** = ~30 feet\n\n**Volume:**\n- **Thimble** = Small amount\n- **Cup** = Handful\n- **Cask** = Large container\n\n**Mass:**\n- **Grain** = Very small\n- **Stone** = Hand-sized\n- **Weight** = Heavy load\n\n---\n\n## MAGIC SYSTEM\n\n### Soulpulse (Resonance)\n\n**Core Principle:** Conscious will given physical form through manipulation of matter's fundamental resonance\n\n**Requirements:**\n1. **Intent** - Clear purpose and focus\n2. **Emotion** - Emotional investment to power the effect\n3. **Cost** - Physical price extracted from the user\n\n**Visibility:** Always visible as colored light corresponding to Principle:\n- **FORM** = Violet light\n- **WHOLENESS** = Amber light\n- **PURITY** = Silver light\n- **ENTROPY** = Red/gray light\n\n### Cost System (Four Stages)\n\n**Stage 1: Strain**\n- Initial fatigue\n- Minor tremors during use\n- Temporary effects\n\n**Stage 2: Fatigue**\n- Persistent tremor baseline\n- Exhaustion after major use\n- Functional impairment begins\n\n**Stage 3: Burnout**\n- Vein-lines permanently visible\n- Fine motor control compromised\n- Recovery takes longer\n\n**Stage 4: Collapse**\n- Complete breakdown\n- Potential death from environmental factors\n- Irreversible without intervention\n\n**Critical Rule:** Direct killing via Soulpulse is extremely difficult. Death usually results from exhaustion combined with environmental hazards.\n\n### Confluence\n\n**Definition:** Simultaneous use of multiple Principles\n\n**Rarity:** Extremely rare, requires intense training or desperation\n\n**Risk:** Collapse (catastrophic overload) if misused\n\n**Jhace's Mastery:** Becomes signature ability, especially after Fathom Break in N05\n\n**Example:** Form + Wholeness = Structural healing with reinforcement\n\n### Power Evolution\n\n**N01-N05 (Emergence):**\n- Basic manipulation\n- Intuitive use\n- High cost\n- Discovery of coordination\n\n**N06-N15 (Training):**\n- Faction techniques\n- Martial applications\n- Combat magic emerges\n\n**N16-N25 (Combat):**\n- Anime-style battle magic\n- Ki/Chakra-like systems\n- Open divine warfare\n\n**N26-N30 (God-Tier):**\n- Planetary-scale effects\n- God confrontations\n- Ultimate mastery\n\n### Limitations\n\n**Three Things Soulpulse CANNOT Do:**\n1. Resurrection of the dead\n2. Time manipulation\n3. True teleportation (spatial manipulation possible but not instant)\n\n---\n\n## STORY STRUCTURE\n\n### Novella Architecture\n\n**Universal Standard (12-Chapter Structure):**\n- **12 numbered chapters** (1,200-2,200 words each)\n- **2 interludes** (600-900 words each)\n- **1 epilogue** (800-1,000 words)\n- **Total:** ~18,000-21,000 words\n\n**Chapter Distribution:**\n- **Act I (Chapters 1-4):** Setup + Crisis (~45% of novella)\n- **Act II (Chapters 5-9):** Pattern + Escalation (~43% of novella)\n- **Act III (Chapters 10-12):** Crisis + Resolution (~25% of novella)\n\n### Beat Unpredictability System\n\n**Core Principle:** No two chapters across the entire 30-novella saga follow the same structural pattern.\n\n**Purpose:** Prevent reader pattern recognition and maintain narrative freshness across 450+ total chapters.\n\n**Beat Catalogue:** 30+ distinct beat methods defining structure (how events unfold), not content.\n\n**Example Beats:**\n- **BEAT_01:** Observation → Pattern Recognition\n- **BEAT_02:** Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n- **BEAT_03:** Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution\n- **BEAT_04:** Investigation → Discovery → Complication\n\n**Tier System:**\n- **Tier 1 (High Impact):** Crisis, revelation, loss, transformation\n- **Tier 2 (Medium Impact):** Investigation, relationship development, preparation\n- **Tier 3 (Low Impact):** Rest, reflection, procedural sequences\n\n**Target Balance Per Novella:**\n- Tier 1: 4-5 chapters\n- Tier 2: 3-4 chapters\n- Tier 3: 3-4 chapters\n\n**Constraints:**\n1. **No consecutive repeats** from immediately prior novella\n2. **No forbidden sequences** - 73+ two-chapter sequences banned from reuse\n3. **Minimum separation** for major beats (Loss, Revelation, Betrayal)\n4. **Emotional balance** - No more than 2 consecutive HIGH or 3 consecutive LOW chapters\n\n### Interlude Structure\n\n**Format:**\n- **2 per novella** (placed between chapters)\n- **600-900 words each**\n- **God-vessel POV** (Auditor-narrated)\n- **Two-segment structure:** Two different god-vessels per interlude\n\n**Types:**\n- **Build-Up:** Shows tension/possibility/gathering forces\n- **Break-Down:** Shows consequences/exhaustion/recognition\n\n**Requirements:**\n- One of each type per novella\n- Never repeat placement from previous 2 novellas\n- Minimum 4 chapters separation\n- Never both in first 5 or last 5 chapters\n\n**Voice Technique:**\n- **Sensory overwhelm** - Higher density than chapter prose\n- **Principle bias** - Each god-vessel perceives through their Principle filter\n- **Innocent frame** - Experience divinity as extreme mortal variation\n- **Dramatic irony** - Readers see divine nature, characters don't\n\n### Epilogue Structure\n\n**Format:**\n- **1 per novella** (after final chapter)\n- **800-1,000 words**\n- **Auditor direct POV** (no narration filter)\n\n**Voice:**\n- **Sardonic tone** - Dark humor, unsympathetic\n- **Intellectual curiosity** - Clinical but engaged observation\n- **Emotional detachment** - No empathy for subjects\n- **Faint contempt** - Slight disdain for mortal limitations\n- **Precision language** - Exact measurements, academic vocabulary\n- **Withholding technique** - References larger patterns without explaining\n\n**Structure:**\n1. **Opening observation** (80-120 words)\n2. **Development cataloging** (300-400 words)\n3. **Assessment section** (150-250 words)\n4. **Closing notation** (70-100 words)\n\n---\n\n## THEMES & ARCS\n\n### Core Themes\n\n#### 1. Craft as Metaphor\n\n**Glass-Making (Turning 1):**\n- Fragile formation, transparent but breakable\n- Tiffani's craft mirrors her perfectionism and vulnerability\n- \"Glass that listens\" - sensitivity to stress and fracture\n\n**Metal-Forging (Turning 3):**\n- Hammering into shape through intense heat\n- Jhace's craft mirrors his approach to problems\n- \"The grip that holds\" - strength through pressure and tempering\n\n**Dual Craft Journey:**\n- Both crafts require understanding stress, heat, and transformation\n- Both serve as metaphors for character growth\n- Both become tools of resistance against divine chaos\n\n#### 2. Partnership vs. Isolation\n\n**Jhace's Isolation:**\n- Carries 23 names of dead from childhood trauma\n- Believes he must save everyone alone\n- Tremor becomes physical manifestation of burden\n\n**Tiffani's Isolation:**\n- Guilt over Marisse's death drives perfectionism\n- Works alone to prevent mistakes\n- Precision becomes penance, not preference\n\n**Load-Bearing System:**\n- They become structural support for each other\n- \"Remove one, both collapse\"\n- Partnership allows them to attempt more than either could alone\n\n**Third Path:**\n- Neither institutional control (RRO) nor divine chaos (gods)\n- Human collaboration as alternative to cosmic forces\n- \"Truth over comfort, people over institutions\"\n\n#### 3. Cost of Power\n\n**Visible Cost:**\n- Every Soulpulse use creates physical markers\n- Tremors, vein-lines, exhaustion - power always has price\n- Cost accumulation forces strategic choices about power use\n\n**Exponential Cost:**\n- Each use costs more than the last\n- Users must learn efficiency or be destroyed\n- \"The wall held. Jhace's grip did not.\"\n\n**Tiffani's Death:**\n- Ultimate cost - burnout from holding impossible structure\n- Her death saves others but destroys her\n- Demonstrates that some costs are too high\n\n**Jhace's Survival:**\n- Must learn to bear cost without being consumed\n- Tiffani's death teaches him limits\n- Her memory becomes his strength and his burden\n\n#### 4. Order vs. Chaos\n\n**Form + Purity (Order):**\n- Structure, separation, judgment, perfection\n- Corlexi and Mobel represent institutional approach\n- RRO embodies this tendency\n\n**Wholeness + Entropy (Chaos):**\n- Connection, dissolution, unity, release\n- Salee and Eries represent boundary dissolution\n- Divine warfare threatens all structure\n\n**Jhace + Tiffani Balance:**\n- Form + Wholeness = Structural healing\n- Neither pure order nor pure chaos\n- Balance requires both holding and healing\n\n**Divine Warfare:**\n- Four principles in cosmic conflict\n- Each god-vessel believes their principle is correct\n- Only balance can prevent universal destruction\n\n#### 5. Observation vs. Action\n\n**Tiffani's Approach:**\n- Measure first, understand patterns\n- Act with precision and forethought\n- \"Stone doesn't lie, neither do these marks\"\n\n**Jhace's Approach:**\n- Act first, hold the structure\n- Measure consequences later\n- \"Sometimes you have to choose who dies\"\n\n**Auditor's Approach:**\n- Observe only, never intervene\n- False facade - actually orchestrating events\n- \"Documentation\" masks manipulation\n\n**Synthesis:**\n- Both observation AND action needed\n- Neither approach sufficient alone\n- Partnership succeeds because they balance each other\n\n### Major Story Arcs\n\n#### Jhace & Tiffani Relationship Arc\n\n**N01: The Grip That Holds**\n- **Chapters 1-2:** Parallel observation (reputation only)\n- **Chapter 3:** First meeting through competence recognition\n- **Chapters 4-5:** Deliberate observation and measurement\n- **Chapter 6:** First coordination (partnership action)\n- **Chapters 7-8:** Trust building through resistance and acceptance\n- **Chapter 9:** First explicit choice of each other over safety\n- **Chapter 10:** Conflict through care (control vs. autonomy)\n- **Chapter 11:** Trauma bonding and vulnerability exchange\n- **Chapter 12:** Commitment formalized through shared principles\n\n**N02-N04: Partnership Deepens**\n- Interdependence solidifies\n- Political pressure mounts from factions\n- Training others in their techniques\n- \"Load-bearing system\" fully established\n\n**N05: Sight of Dying Light**\n- Tiffani dies holding catastrophic structure\n- Her death triggers Jhace's Fathom Break\n- Fathom Break breaks final seal on gods\n- Jhace survives but is fundamentally changed\n\n#### God Awakening Arc\n\n**Stage 0: Unaware Exceptional (N01-N05)**\n- Gods exist as mortals, unaware of true nature\n- Feel drawn to Soulpulse naturally\n- Experience memory fragments in dreams\n- Fathom Break in N05 breaks final seal → ~30% awakening\n\n**Stage 1: Suspicion Rising (N06-N12)**\n- 30-40% consciousness\n- Fragmentary divine memories\n- Beginning to suspect true nature\n- Faction formation mirrors Principle alignments\n\n**Stage 2: Recognition Without Understanding (N13-N17)**\n- Recognize they're not human in ordinary sense\n- Don't yet know they're literal gods\n- Understand Principle bias is intrinsic\n- Begin sensing cosmic scope\n\n**Stage 3: Awakening to Divine Nature (N18-N25)**\n- Learn divine true names (N17 triggers)\n- Understand they are fragments of larger entities\n- Remember pieces of pre-incarnation existence\n- Comprehend their role in cosmic conflict\n\n**Stage 4: Full Divine Awareness (N26-N30)**\n- Complete understanding of identity and purpose\n- Memory of ancient divine war restored\n- Recognition of Counter-Spark's orchestration\n- Preparation for final confrontation\n\n#### Soulpulse Evolution Arc\n\n**Emergence (N01):**\n- First use during Piercing crisis\n- Accidental activation through desperate need\n- Discovery of coordination potential\n\n**Training (N02-N15):**\n- Faction techniques developed\n- Martial applications emerge\n- Combat magic begins to appear\n\n**Combat (N16-N25):**\n- Anime-style battle magic fully emerges\n- Ki/Chakra-like systems develop\n- Open divine warfare across Te'Oga\n\n**God-Tier (N26-N30):**\n- Planetary-scale confrontations\n- Ultimate mastery achieved\n- Cosmic-level stakes\n\n#### Auditor Deception Arc\n\n**Facade (N01-N25):**\n- Appears as supportive observer\n- Documents god awakening \"celebration\"\n- Subtly orchestrates events toward god empowerment\n- Maintains mask of neutrality\n\n**Manipulation:**\n- Interludes provide \"helpful\" context that actually serves her goals\n- Epilogues assess progress toward god awakening\n- Never directly intervenes but influences through observation\n\n**Revelation (N26-N30):**\n- True identity as Counter-Spark revealed\n- Goal is elimination of gods, not celebration\n- Every previous \"observation\" gains new hostile meaning\n- Final confrontation with Jhace and remaining forces\n\n---\n\n## NARRATIVE RULES\n\n### Knowledge Gates\n\n**Dual-Canon System:**\n- **Innocent Frame (N01-N29):** Only information revealed to characters/readers\n- **Guilty Frame (N30):** Complete cosmological truth\n\n**Mechanical Enforcement:**\n- Each fact has specific novella when it unlocks\n- Prevents accidental spoilers across 30-volume scope\n- Allows intricate long-term plotting while keeping immediate story clean\n\n**Example Gates:**\n- **N17:** Public god revelation\n- **N26+:** Counter-Spark identity revealed\n- **N30:** Complete cosmological truth\n\n### Lexicon Enforcement\n\n**Forbidden Terms:**\n- Earth time units (hour, day, week, month, year, Monday, January)\n- Earth measurements (inch, foot, meter, mile)\n- Modern slang (okay, yeah, cool, guys)\n- Anachronistic language\n- Earth cultural references\n\n**Approved Terms:**\n- Te'Oga time units (Tide, Anneal, Crucible, Turn, Cast, Turning)\n- Te'Oga measurements (Span, Stride, Reach, Throw)\n- Soulpulse terminology (Resonance, Soulpulse, The Force, The Power)\n- Craft-based language grounded in physical reality\n\n### Cost Visibility\n\n**Rule:** Every Soulpulse use must show visible physical consequences\n\n**Markers:**\n- Tremors (Stage 1-2)\n- Vein-lines (Stage 2-3)\n- Exhaustion (all stages)\n- Sensory overload (approaching Stage 4)\n- Functional impairment (fine motor control, grip strength)\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Prevents power without consequence\n- Forces strategic choices about power use\n- Creates tension and stakes\n- Visual representation of character deterioration\n\n### Beat Unpredictability\n\n**Core Principle:** No chapter patterns repeat across 30 novellas\n\n**Purpose:**\n- Maintain narrative freshness across 450+ chapters\n- Prevent reader pattern recognition\n- Force creative problem-solving per novella\n- Allow thematic consistency without structural repetition\n\n**Implementation:**\n- 30+ distinct beat methods in catalogue\n- Constraint system prevents pattern formation\n- Audit tracker documents all usage\n- Forbidden sequences grow by ~15 per novella\n\n### Innocent Frame\n\n**Definition:** Characters don't know they're gods - interpret divinity as extreme talent\n\n**Technique:**\n- Show readers divine nature through experiences\n- Have characters rationalize through mortal frameworks\n- Maintain dramatic irony (readers know more)\n- Gradual revelation across saga\n\n**Examples:**\n- \"I've always been more sensitive than others\"\n- \"My Soulpulse capacity seems unusually high\"\n- \"I feel drawn toward [Principle-aligned activities]\"\n- Never: \"I am a god\" or \"I am fragment of [Divine Name]\"\n\n---\n\n## TIMELINE\n\n### Turning 1: GLASS (N01-N05)\n\n**Theme:** Fragile formation, first emergence, transparent but breakable\n\n**N01: The Grip That Holds**\n- **Timeline:** Tide 0 (Piercing) through Tide 20\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Piercing event (Ch 3)\n  - Jhace-Tiffani first meeting\n  - Soulpulse discovery\n  - Partnership formation\n  - Three priorities written on forge wall\n\n**N02: Lines Under Pressure**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 20-40\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Political pressure from factions\n  - RRO confrontation\n  - Tiffani learns Soulpulse\n  - Partnership deepens\n  - First major coordinated holds\n\n**N03: Aftershock**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 40-60\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Faction formation accelerates\n  - Sabotage reveals internal conflicts\n  - Chloen training continues\n  - Bram's memory triggers action\n  - Network building begins\n\n**N04: The Slow Melt**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 60-80\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Betrayal and isolation\n  - Apprentice success (Chloen milestone)\n  - Moral compromise required\n  - Divine forces become more visible\n  - Cost accumulation accelerates\n\n**N05: Sight of Dying Light**\n- **Timeline:** ~Tides 80-100\n- **Key Events:**\n  - Tiffani's death (Ch 14)\n  - Jhace's Fathom Break\n  - Final seal broken on gods\n  - God awakening to ~30% consciousness\n  - Saga shifts to cosmic scale\n\n### Turning 2: CRACK (N06-N10)\n\n**Theme:** Collision of forces, fracture lines appearing, pressure mounting\n\n**God Consciousness:** 30-40% awakening, fragmentary divine memories\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Faction formation around Principle alignments\n- Open conflict between institutions and independents\n- God-vessels begin suspecting true nature\n- Divine warfare begins on mortal proxy level\n\n### Turning 3: FORGE (N11-N15)\n\n**Theme:** Hammering into shape, intense heat, transformation begins\n\n**God Consciousness:** 80-100% awakening, full divine memory restored\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Gods go public (N17)\n- Open divine warfare across Te'Oga\n- Mortals caught between cosmic forces\n- Jhace begins developing lattice framework\n- Anime-style battle magic fully emerges\n\n### Turning 4: SMELT (N16-N20)\n\n**Theme:** Melting down, purification, separation of dross\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- N17: Public god revelation\n- Full-scale divine warfare\n- Jhace realizes balance is only solution\n- Lattice system begins development\n- Cost of power becomes critical issue\n\n### Turning 5: CLEAR (N21-N25)\n\n**Theme:** Refinement, clarity emerging, truth visible\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Lattice system developed (managed opposition)\n- Gods reluctantly accept compromise\n- Hints of opposition to balance emerge\n- Framework for coexistence tested\n- Jhace reaches mastery level\n\n### Turning 6: BREAK (N26-N30)\n\n**Theme:** Final shattering, ultimate transformation, complete revelation\n\n**Status:** [RESERVED - Final confrontation and resolution]\n\n**Key Developments:**\n- Counter-Spark identity revealed\n- Final confrontation and resolution\n- Complete cosmological truths revealed\n- Saga conclusion\n\n---\n\n## APPENDICES\n\n### Appendix A: Beat Method Examples\n\n**BEAT_01: Observation → Pattern Recognition**\n- **Structure:** Establish baseline POV → Notice anomaly → Recognize pattern\n- **Emotional Arc:** Low intensity\n- **Example:** Chapter 1-2 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Establish character expertise and introduce conflict\n\n**BEAT_02: Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost**\n- **Structure:** Attempt solution → Fail → Try again with higher stakes → Succeed with cost\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium to High\n- **Example:** Chapter 4 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Demonstrate learning and cost accumulation\n\n**BEAT_03: Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution**\n- **Structure:** Immediate crisis → Escalating problems → Narrow escape with cost\n- **Emotional Arc:** High intensity\n- **Example:** Chapter 3 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Major turning point, high stakes action\n\n**BEAT_04: Investigation → Discovery → Complication**\n- **Structure:** Gather information → Find truth → Truth creates new problems\n- **Emotional Arc:** Medium intensity\n- **Example:** Chapter 8 of N01\n- **Purpose:** Knowledge revelation that advances plot\n\n### Appendix B: Interconnected Document System\n\n**Core Canon Documents:**\n- Saga Blueprint\n- Innocent Frame Canon\n- Guilty Frame Canon\n- Knowledge Gate Progression\n\n**Constraint Enforcement:**\n- Beat Unpredictability System\n- N01-N05 Beat Sequence Audit Tracker\n- Interlude Placement Progression Tracker\n- Non-Chapter Validator\n\n**Production Templates:**\n- Story Bible Template Structure\n- Chapter Generation Template\n- Interlude Generation Template\n- Epilogue Generation Template\n\n**Reference Materials:**\n- Character State Tables\n- Faction Context Maps\n- Timeline Trackers\n- Lexicon Guides\n\n### Appendix C: Production Workflow\n\n**Bible Generation:**\n1. Load all constraints and prior context\n2. Establish novella parameters and position\n3. Assign chapter beats (verify against tracker)\n4. Place interludes (avoid previous positions)\n5. Fill all 14 template sections\n6. Run quality checkpoints\n7. Deliver complete bible\n\n**Prose Generation:**\n1. Receive complete bible\n2. Generate 12 chapters following beat structures\n3. Maintain lexicon and style compliance\n4. Show cost visibility throughout\n5. Respect knowledge gates\n6. Stay within word count targets\n\n**Integration Process:**\n1. Insert interludes at designated positions\n2. Add minor line edits for connection seeding\n3. Generate epilogue\n4. Run final quality verification\n5. Archive completed novella\n\n---\n\n## CONCLUSION\n\nThe OmniMeta Saga represents a meticulously crafted narrative universe spanning 30 novellas and 450+ chapters. Every element - from the dual craft metaphors to the beat unpredictability system - serves the central themes of partnership, cost, and balance.\n\nThe story follows Jhace Torrins from a craftsman seeking to save everyone to a leader who must choose who to save, ultimately confronting cosmic forces that threaten universal existence. His partnership with Tiffani Merrow provides both the strength to continue and the vulnerability that makes him human.\n\nThe four god-vessels awakening to their divine nature represent different approaches to power and order, while the hidden antagonist (The Auditor/Counter-Spark) orchestrates events toward stasis and destruction. Only through understanding the cost of power and the necessity of balance can Jhace prevent the complete elimination of divine forces from the universe.\n\nThe narrative techniques - innocent frame, beat unpredictability, knowledge gates, lexicon enforcement - all serve to maintain dramatic tension across the massive scope while ensuring each novella provides a satisfying, complete reading experience.\n\nThis story bible serves as the definitive reference for the entire saga, containing all canonical information, character profiles, world mechanics, and production guidelines needed to understand and continue the narrative.\n\n---\n\n**Document Version:** 1.0  \n**Last Updated:** December 2025  \n**Status:** Complete Saga Documentation  \n**Authority:** Definitive Reference for OmniMeta Saga  \n\n**END OF STORY BIBLE**"
          ]
        },

          "text": "4301\n language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments\n"

          "parts": [
            "Validation: ✓ APPROVED FOR INCLUSION\n\nThis Chapter 1 prose is production-ready and fully compliant with the N01 Story Bible constraints:\n\nStructural Compliance ✓\nWord Count: ~1,850 words (within 1,200-2,200 target)\n\nPOV: Jhace (tight third-person limited) ✓\n\nTide: 0, Kindling ✓\n\nSetting: Underface beneath Saint Merro ✓\n\nBeat Execution: BEAT_01 (Observation → Pattern Recognition) perfectly executed\n\nOpens with routine expertise\n\nDetects wrongness through sensory precision\n\nDocuments pattern (chalk marks, slate notes)\n\nEnds with forward momentum\n\nLexicon & Style ✓\nZero Earth terms: Uses Tide, Kindling, spans, Underface correctly ✓\n\nForge-punk language: Gridwright expertise, load paths, brackets, stress lines integrated naturally ✓\n\nVoice: Deep POV, past tense, no head-hopping ✓\n\nSensory density: Heat, vibration, stone texture, cold bands all grounded ✓\n\nCanon Compliance ✓\nCost visibility: Pre-Piercing (no Soulpulse yet), but tremor foreshadowing established ✓\n\nRelationship: Bram's caretaker dynamic present without exposition ✓\n\nTrauma foundation: \"Twenty-three bodies\" count referenced, establishing Jhace's wound ✓\n\nSetting anchor: Underface tunnels established as load-bearing character ✓\n\nKnowledge gate: No divine truth, only structural wrongness ✓\n\nEmotional intensity: Low (as required), building tension without crisis ✓\n\nQuality Markers\nOpening hook: Immediate sensory immersion\n\nCharacter voice: Jhace's competence and vigilance clear\n\nWorld-building: Te'Oga's post-Piercing instability shown, not told\n\nLast line: \"Work first,\" he said, and began the next check — perfect forward momentum\n\nStatus: CHAPTER 1 LOCKED ✓\n\nReady for: Chapter 2 Prose Generation (\"Glass That Listens\" - Tiffani POV)"
          ]
        },

    "94efaa74-8f17-416b-9a38-f8d18ec0c058": {
      "children": [
        "bceb353f-4c5c-4842-a0f2-b90f9fc8c019"
      ],
      "id": "94efaa74-8f17-416b-9a38-f8d18ec0c058",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": null,
          "role": "assistant"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "code",
          "language": "unknown",
          "response_format_name": null,
          "text": "for ch in range(1,13):\n    txt=sections[f\"CH{ch:02d}\"]\n    # find POV line\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*POV:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    pov=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    # extract names in summary perhaps\n    summary=txt.split(\"**Summary:**\",1)[1] if \"**Summary:**\" in txt else \"\"\n    # list capitalized names occurrences\n    names=set(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}\\b', summary))\n    # filter some stopwords\n    stop={\"Summary\",\"Key\",\"Beats\",\"Soulpulse\",\"Stage\",\"Progression\",\"Cost\",\"Visuals\",\"Requirements\",\"Relationship\",\"Status\",\"Piercing\",\"Underface\",\"Saint\",\"Merro\",\"Tide\",\"Pouring\",\"Shaping\",\"Striking\",\"Quench\",\"Kindling\",\"Act\"}\n    names=[n for n in names if n not in stop]\n    print(ch, pov, sorted(names)[:10])\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797094.802609,
        "end_turn": false,
        "id": "94efaa74-8f17-416b-9a38-f8d18ec0c058",

    "96c2f6fe-9eb5-443e-afb8-83ad0fef86fc": {
      "children": [
        "1d6bf8d6-fb13-416f-a038-e016d6de2282"
      ],
      "id": "96c2f6fe-9eb5-443e-afb8-83ad0fef86fc",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "file_search",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "multimodal_text",
          "parts": [
            "Make sure to include fileciteturn1file0 in your response to cite this file, or to surface it as a link.",
            "# N01 – Tiffani Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**\nDefines scene- and sentence-level voice for **Tiffani POV** in N01, using Chapter 2 (\"Glass That Listens\") as the baseline.\n\nUse this whenever writing or revising any tight-third scene from Tiffani's perspective.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Tiffani Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. Narration is aligned to Tiffani's mind: what she notices, how she frames it, and how she reasons.\n\n**Her mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Patterns and deviations.**\n   * The world presents as data: measurements, trends, variance from expected behavior.\n   * She's always asking: Is this within range? Is this noise or signal?\n\n2. **System implications.**\n   * She leaps from a small anomaly to its possible downstream effects.\n   * Thoughts often move: observation → measurement → consequence.\n\n3. **Self-audited thinking.**\n   * She questions her own conclusions in real time.\n   * She labels states: tentative, baseline, pattern, noise.\n\n4. **Emotion behind control.**\n   * Care and fear are present but wrapped inside procedure and analysis.\n   * She rarely names feelings directly; she describes their physical footprint and impact on reasoning.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nAnalytical, layered, more verbal than Jhace but still lean. Sentences can run longer, especially where she stacks conditions or tracks causality.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cognitive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n   * Instrument status: lens clarity, hairlines in glass, alignment, scale marks.\n\n2. **Measurement / abstract sense**\n   * Subdivision counts, ranges, variance, repetition over Tides.\n   * She pairs sensory input with a mental number or range: \"within ± one subdivision.\"\n\n3. **Kinesthetic / touch**\n   * Hands on stone, instruments, glass.\n   * Physical confirmation: stand feet on stone, lack of grit, coolness/warmth.\n\n4. **Taste / internal sensation**\n   * Metallic taste as an early warning.\n   * Mouth, tongue, heartbeat, tension.\n\n5. **Sound**\n   * Humming glass, kiln exhale, city noise; used more as context and escalation than baseline.\n\n**Guideline:**\nIn important beats, pair the sensory detail with a measurement concept (e.g., \"Two subdivisions high and holding,\" \"small but compounding shift\").\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lexicon & Global Constraints\n\nSame world constraints apply:\n\n* **No Earth units or references.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Quench, Anneal, spans, etc.\n\n* **No metaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my choice to call signal noise.\"\n  * \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\nAvoid indulgent grief monologues. Her guilt is real but channeled into stricter process.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Tiffani\n\nWhen Tiffani speaks:\n\n* **More words than Jhace, but still efficient.**\n\n* **Often clarifying or reframing:**\n  * \"It isn't a pastime. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but stay controlled.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Anti-Repetition Strategy\n\nWe want to keep her technical language consistent, but avoid distracting repetition in tight spans.\n\n### 8.1 Core measurement terms\n\nThese form a small controlled set:\n\n* **noise, signal, pattern, drift, deviation, shift, range, baseline, anomaly, state, source, external, internal, compounding, persistent.**\n\n**Rules:**\n\n* Within a single paragraph, avoid repeating the exact same keyword more than twice unless it's deliberate emphasis (e.g., everything revolves around noise vs signal in a short burst).\n* Over a scene, allow consistent reuse of key terms; she is a specialist with a tight lexicon.\n\n### 8.2 Instrument terms\n\nKeep technical clarity first; vary only where it doesn't confuse:\n\n* **Theodolite:** instrument, scope, tube, stand depending on context.\n* **Glass:** prism, pane, lens, plate, glass.\n* **Logging:** logbook, record, entry, column.\n\nDon't force synonyms that weaken the sense of a professional, repeatable process.\n\n---\n\n## 9. Tiffani Scene Template\n\nFor most Tiffani scenes, you can loosely follow this pattern:\n\n1. **Instrument / environment check**\n   * She runs through tools or conditions in a defined order.\n   * Establish baseline: what \"normal\" looks like.\n\n2. **Initial anomaly**\n   * Small deviation in measurement.\n   * She notes it, records it, labels it (tentative, minor, within range).\n\n3. **Pattern recognition**\n   * She compares to previous Tides or entries.\n   * \"Pattern appears persistent\" moment.\n\n4. **Social friction**\n   * Someone interrupts: guild messenger, council voice, Pella.\n   * Conflict between \"urgent visible tasks\" and her \"invisible risk\" concerns.\n\n5. **Escalation of anomaly**\n   * Deviation increases; new signals appear (glass vibration, taste, visual changes).\n   * She re-tests, re-validates instrument, rules out her own error.\n\n6. **Trauma echo / methodological vow**\n   * Brief, concrete reference to Marisse and what she misclassified.\n   * Recommitment: will not call this noise.\n\n7. **Decision**\n   * Document the state.\n   * Choose who to tell or where to go next.\n   * Small physical exit beat (moving toward hatch, stair, council, etc.)\n\n---\n\n## 10. Quick \"Voice Check\" Phrases\n\nIf a passage feels off, check against these example lines from Chapter 2:\n\n**Analysis & Self-Correction:**\n* \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n* \"Persistent meant repeatable. Repeatable meant real. Real meant it could hurt someone.\"\n* \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n**Marisse Trauma (Methodological):**\n* \"Noise killed Marisse. Or her decision to call signal noise.\"\n* \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\n**Conditional Logic:**\n* \"When I have more than two points of drift and some glass humming, I'll speak to the council.\"\n\n**Dry Humor (with Pella):**\n* \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n* \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n* \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n\n**Self-Instruction:**\n* \"Document, then move.\"\n* \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n* \"New state. Document, then move.\"\n\n**Physical Manifestation of Emotion:**\n* \"Her pulse picked up. Not fear. Yet. Just the sense of a pattern gaining speed.\"\n* \"Her hand shook once as she lifted the pen. She braced her wrist on the stand.\"\n\nIf the prose could naturally accommodate lines like these without clashing, you're in the right voice space for Tiffani.\n\n---\n\n## 11. Usage Notes\n\n**File Location:** \nPlace in `/canon/` folder alongside `N01_Jhace_Prose_Voice_Guide.md` and `Character_Dossiers_N01.md`, or create a `/style/` folder for prose-specific guides.\n\n**When to Load:**\nAny prose engine (Custom GPT, Claude, Perplexity) working on Tiffani POV chapters should load this document alongside:\n- Character_Dossiers_N01.md\n- Relationship_Dynamics_N01.md\n- World_Core.md\n- Constraint_Systems.md\n- Relevant chapter grid specifications\n\n**Validation:**\nUse this guide as the line-level standard during chapter revision passes. Check each Tiffani scene against Sections 2-3 (rhythm, sensory hierarchy) and Section 8 (repetition avoidance).\n\n**Comparison to Jhace:**\nTiffani's voice is distinct from Jhace's in key ways:\n- Longer sentences (10-22 words vs. 7-18)\n- Visual/optical sense prioritized vs. touch/vibration\n- Measurement vocabulary vs. material vocabulary\n- Self-auditing thought process vs. terse declarations\n- More dialogue words, conditional framing vs. few words, instructive\n\n---\n\n**Version:** 1.0 (Locked to Chapter 2: \"Glass That Listens\" as reference standard)\n"
          ]
        },
        "create_time": 1765860054.2534869,
        "end_turn": null,

          "text": "# N01 – Tiffani Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**\nDefines scene- and sentence-level voice for **Tiffani POV** in N01, using Chapter 2 (\"Glass That Listens\") as the baseline.\n\nUse this whenever writing or revising any tight-third scene from Tiffani's perspective.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Tiffani Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. Narration is aligned to Tiffani's mind: what she notices, how she frames it, and how she reasons.\n\n**Her mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Patterns and deviations.**\n   * The world presents as data: measurements, trends, variance from expected behavior.\n   * She's always asking: Is this within range? Is this noise or signal?\n\n2. **System implications.**\n   * She leaps from a small anomaly to its possible downstream effects.\n   * Thoughts often move: observation → measurement → consequence.\n\n3. **Self-audited thinking.**\n   * She questions her own conclusions in real time.\n   * She labels states: tentative, baseline, pattern, noise.\n\n4. **Emotion behind control.**\n   * Care and fear are present but wrapped inside procedure and analysis.\n   * She rarely names feelings directly; she describes their physical footprint and impact on reasoning.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nAnalytical, layered, more verbal than Jhace but still lean. Sentences can run longer, especially where she stacks conditions or tracks causality.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cogni\n"
        },

                  "name": "stdout",
                  "text": "# N01 – Tiffani Prose Voice & Line-Level Style Guide\n\n**Scope:**\nDefines scene- and sentence-level voice for **Tiffani POV** in N01, using Chapter 2 (\"Glass That Listens\") as the baseline.\n\nUse this whenever writing or revising any tight-third scene from Tiffani's perspective.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Core POV: How Tiffani Thinks\n\n**Mode:** Tight third-person limited. Narration is aligned to Tiffani's mind: what she notices, how she frames it, and how she reasons.\n\n**Her mental priorities:**\n\n1. **Patterns and deviations.**\n   * The world presents as data: measurements, trends, variance from expected behavior.\n   * She's always asking: Is this within range? Is this noise or signal?\n\n2. **System implications.**\n   * She leaps from a small anomaly to its possible downstream effects.\n   * Thoughts often move: observation → measurement → consequence.\n\n3. **Self-audited thinking.**\n   * She questions her own conclusions in real time.\n   * She labels states: tentative, baseline, pattern, noise.\n\n4. **Emotion behind control.**\n   * Care and fear are present but wrapped inside procedure and analysis.\n   * She rarely names feelings directly; she describes their physical footprint and impact on reasoning.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Sentence Rhythm & Diction\n\n**Overall texture:**\nAnalytical, layered, more verbal than Jhace but still lean. Sentences can run longer, especially where she stacks conditions or tracks causality.\n\n### Sentence rules\n\n* **Default length:** Medium; 10–22 words is common.\n\n* **Short sentences for:**\n  * Conclusions: \"She did not call it noise now.\"\n  * Observed facts: \"Her teeth tasted of metal.\"\n\n* **Long sentences for:**\n  * Describing procedures.\n  * Walking through cause and effect.\n  * Mapping patterns and implications.\n\n### Diction rules\n\n* **Core vocabulary is measurement and classification:**\n  * phase, refraction, deviation, subdivision, baseline, pattern, noise, drift, range, stability, gradient, source, state, external, internal, compounding.\n\n* **Verbs tend to be cogni\n"

        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "### **CHAPTER 9: \"Ash and Claims\"**\n- **Tide:** 10-12\n- **Setting:** Glass Quarter refugee camp, Crown administrative contact\n- **Beat Method:** BEAT_16 (Investigation → Discovery → Complication)\n- **Emotional Intensity:** Medium\n- **Target Word Count:** ~1,700 words\n- **Status:** TO BE WRITTEN\n\n**Summary:**\n\n**Key Beats:**\n- Corvin/Tellis offer (resources for control)\n- She consults Jhace (first time asking partner input)\n- His question: what do YOU want?\n- Her honest answer: contradictory desires\n- His commitment: vulnerable together\n- Public refusal of institutional power\n- Exit: political enemies made, personal bond strengthened\n\n**Soulpulse:** None (political chapter)\n\n**Relationship:** First explicit choice of each other over safety/pragmatism\n\n**Stage Progression:** Stable Stage 1 with coordinated cost management\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797109.4627056,

    "9c6d7b84-80c7-43fd-b124-b02f808ea5f9": {
      "children": [
        "1d984aae-7646-4327-b237-3ded9f1ed5a2"
      ],
      "id": "9c6d7b84-80c7-43fd-b124-b02f808ea5f9",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "BEAT_01 through BEAT_08.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Baseline Observation**  \n   - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n   - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n\n2. **Focused Attention**  \n   - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n   - Compares against prior experience.\n\n3. **Testing / Probing**  \n   - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n   - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n\n4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n   - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n   - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n\n5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n   - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nCuriosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Introducing a core problem.  \n- Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n- Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n- Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n\n---\n\n\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765861336.0417452,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "9c6d7b84-80c7-43fd-b124-b02f808ea5f9",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "# find BEAT_01 details\nm = re.search(r\"BEAT_01.*?(?=### BEAT_02|## BEAT_02|### BEAT_2|# BEAT_02)\", beat_text, flags=re.S)\nif m:\n    print(m.group(0)[:2200])\nelse:\n    # fallback search\n    idx = beat_text.find(\"BEAT_01\")\n    print(beat_text[idx:idx+2200])\n",
            "end_time": 1765861336.3204587,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
            "jupyter_messages": [

                "content": {

    "a5a7678b-8fc9-4dd6-b68f-b7511097554b": {
      "children": [
        "f62688b6-0353-4569-979b-17e1fd1a68c1"
      ],
      "id": "a5a7678b-8fc9-4dd6-b68f-b7511097554b",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "tive or technical rather than purely physical:**\n  * check, confirm, measure, compare, project, classify, adjust, repeat, record, conclude.\n\n* **Physical verbs remain simple and clear:**\n  * lean, tap, press, steady, tilt, set, lift.\n\n* **Avoid poetic or decorative phrasing.** Her language is clear and precise rather than ornate.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Sensory Hierarchy & Immersion\n\nTiffani is less body-first than Jhace, but still strongly sensory, especially around glass and light.\n\n**Priority for description:**\n\n1. **Visual / optical**\n   * Star positions, arcs, rings, curves, lines, brightness, smear, color shift.\n   * Instrument status: lens clarity, hairlines in glass, alignment, scale marks.\n\n2. **Measurement / abstract sense**\n   * Subdivision counts, ranges, variance, repetition over Tides.\n   * She pairs sensory input with a mental number or range: \"within ± one subdivision.\"\n\n3. **Kinesthetic / touch**\n   * Hands on stone, instruments, glass.\n   * Physical confirmation: stand feet on stone, lack of grit, coolness/warmth.\n\n4. **Taste / internal sensation**\n   * Metallic taste as an early warning.\n   * Mouth, tongue, heartbeat, tension.\n\n5. **Sound**\n   * Humming glass, kiln exhale, city noise; used more as context and escalation than baseline.\n\n**Guideline:**\nIn important beats, pair the sensory detail with a measurement concept (e.g., \"Two subdivisions high and holding,\" \"small but compounding shift\").\n\n---\n\n## 4. Lexicon & Global Constraints\n\nSame world constraints apply:\n\n* **No Earth units or references.**\n  * Use: Tide, Kindling, Pouring, Quench, Anneal, spans, etc.\n\n* **No metaphors or similes.**\n  * No \"like,\" \"as if,\" \"as though\" for comparisons.\n  * No figurative comparisons at all; describe observed properties directly.\n\n* **No strong personification.**\n  * Avoid language that gives intent to inanimate things.\n  * Acceptable: \"The kiln vented,\" \"The glass vibrated.\"\n  * Avoid: \"The sky tried to hide something,\" \"The oven begged to be heard.\"\n\n* **Use Te'Oga-\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860317.8953385,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "a5a7678b-8fc9-4dd6-b68f-b7511097554b",

        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "## BEAT CATALOG — N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine all beat methods used in N01 so chapters can be:\n\n- Planned and validated structurally.  \n- Kept unpredictable at the series level.  \n- Emotionally coherent within themselves.\n\nN01 uses **eight** beat methods: BEAT_01 through BEAT_08.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Baseline Observation**  \n   - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n   - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n\n2. **Focused Attention**  \n   - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n   - Compares against prior experience.\n\n3. **Testing / Probing**  \n   - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n   - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n\n4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n   - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n   - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n\n5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n   - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nCuriosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Introducing a core problem.  \n- Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n- Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n- Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when you want a **hard failure** that escalates, followed by a victory that hurts.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Trial Setup**  \n   - Goal is defined (save someone, stabilize structure, train Soulpulse).  \n   - Plan seems plausible but not guaranteed.\n\n2. **Initial Attempt → Failure**\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860355.5610163,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "a81a4954-51b0-4584-8a19-99a1f2c758fb",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "beat = Path(\"/mnt/data/Beat_Catalog_N01_Scope.md\").read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\")\nprint(beat[:2000])\n",
            "end_time": 1765860355.9035413,

                  "text": "## BEAT CATALOG — N01 SCOPE\n\n**Purpose:**  \nDefine all beat methods used in N01 so chapters can be:\n\n- Planned and validated structurally.  \n- Kept unpredictable at the series level.  \n- Emotionally coherent within themselves.\n\nN01 uses **eight** beat methods: BEAT_01 through BEAT_08.\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_01 — Observation → Pattern Recognition\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when the chapter is about **noticing something wrong** and understanding it.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Baseline Observation**  \n   - POV engages in normal work/routine.  \n   - Small detail feels off (vibration, light behavior, behavior pattern).\n\n2. **Focused Attention**  \n   - POV chooses to pay attention instead of ignoring.  \n   - Compares against prior experience.\n\n3. **Testing / Probing**  \n   - Small actions to test the anomaly.  \n   - Additional observations confirm the anomaly is real.\n\n4. **Pattern Recognition**  \n   - Click moment: “This is not random.”  \n   - POV understands the pattern (or understands that a pattern exists and is dangerous).\n\n5. **Decision / Micro-Shift**  \n   - POV changes course or plan based on the new understanding.\n\n**Emotional Arc:**  \nCuriosity → Unease → Focus → Understanding → Resolve\n\n**Best Used When:**\n- Introducing a core problem.  \n- Showing a character’s core mode of thinking (Jhace sensing structures, Tiffani reading patterns).\n\n**Forbidden Sequences:**\n- Cannot immediately follow another BEAT_01.  \n- Cannot immediately precede BEAT_06 (both are recognition/synthesis-heavy).\n\n**N01 Examples (intended):**\n- Ch 1 (Jhace: “Something Wrong” under the heat-grid).  \n- Ch 2 (Tiffani: observing upper atmosphere anomalies).\n\n---\n\n### BEAT_02 — Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost\n\n**Function:**  \nUse when you want a **hard failure** that escalates, followed by a victory that hurts.\n\n**Structure:**\n\n1. **Trial Setup**  \n   -     "a82c3fb9-43de-4f38-ae4a-8116680a2be0": {
      "children": [
        "58189359-83a4-4858-820c-e10a5b979771"
      ],
      "id": "a82c3fb9-43de-4f38-ae4a-8116680a2be0",

          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "'# N01 BEAT MAPPING LAYER\\n**Translation Document Between Local and Saga-Level Beat Systems**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## PURPOSE\\n\\nThis document translates between:\\n- **N01 Local Beat IDs (BEAT_01–BEAT_08):** Used by validators and prose generation\\n- **Saga Beat IDs (Beat 1–30+):** Used for cross-novella unpredictability tracking\\n\\n**Rule of Precedence:**\\n- Prose generation + validation → Use **N01 local beats** and N01 constraints\\n- Saga tracking → Record **Saga Beat ID** for cross-novella pattern prevention\\n\\n---\\n\\n## CORE MAPPING TABLE\\n\\n| N01 Local Beat | N01 Beat Name (Canon) | Saga Beat ID | Saga Beat Name |\\n|----------------|----------------------|--------------|----------------|\\n| **BEAT_01** | Observation → Pattern Recognition | **Beat 10** | Observation → Pattern Recognition |\\n| **BEAT_02** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost | **Beat 1** | Trial → Failure → Compounding → Success/Cost |\\n| **BEAT_03** | Crisis Front-Loaded → Rolling Complications → Partial Resolution | **Beat 3** | External Threat → Narrow Escape |\\n| **BEAT_04** | Quiet Recalibration → Offer → Refusal/Choice | **Beat 16** | Investigation → Discovery → Complication *(primary mapping for discovery-driven choices)* |\\n| **B'"
        },
        "create_time": 1765796949.4813254,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "a82c3fb9-43de-4f38-ae4a-8116680a2be0",

    "a8e02ccf-fe4f-459a-a0d4-264a928fcbd7": {
      "children": [
        "f82b02d5-ccac-49a7-945d-bcaf804f0484"
      ],
      "id": "a8e02ccf-fe4f-459a-a0d4-264a928fcbd7",
      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "specific social and work language:** guild, Crown, Glass Quarter, Underface, council, ovens, platforms, Anneals, etc.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Detail Level: Where to Zoom In\n\n### Zoom in deeply when:\n\n* Tiffani is:\n  * Running a measurement sequence.\n  * Identifying a pattern or a break in pattern.\n  * Recalling Marisse's accident and its methodological implications.\n  * Interpreting the sky or glass behavior.\n\n**Techniques when zoomed in:**\n\n* Break the procedure into named steps:\n  * Check instrument → confirm baseline → record deviation → label conclusion (tentative/final).\n\n* Explicitly mark logic:\n  * \"If it repeats under controlled adjustment, it's real. If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Internal Thought Style\n\n**Tiffani's internal thoughts:**\n\n* Often in short, declarative fragments, used to label or correct.\n  * \"Not fear. Yet.\"\n  * \"Sky-things. Efficient.\"\n\n* Frequently self-instructive:\n  * \"Repeat. If it repeats, it's real.\"\n  * \"Document, then move.\"\n\n* Emotion appears as diagnosed state:\n  * She may note pulse, tension, shaking hands, then classify it: adrenaline, anticipation.\n\n* She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my choice to call signal noise.\"\n  * \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\nAvoid indulgent grief monologues. Her guilt is real but channeled into stricter process.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Tiffani\n\nWhen Tiffani speaks:\n\n* **More words than Jhace, but still efficient.**\n\n* **Often clarifying or reframing:**\n  * \"It isn't a pastime. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible. I think they lack resolution.\"\n\n* **She avoids direct emotional admissions, especially to authority.** With Pella, the edges soften but\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860322.6339543,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "a8e02ccf-fe4f-459a-a0d4-264a928fcbd7",

If not, look for your own error first.\"\n\n### Zoom out when:\n\n* She traverses physical space without new information (stairs, corridors).\n* Routine work proceeds without anomaly.\n* Interactions with others are simple confirmations or status reports.\n\nUse a few clean sentences to summarize and jump to the next point where data changes.\n\n---\n\n## 6. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based jokes: \"Everyone downstairs says you're impossible.         "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "She references Marisse's death in methodological terms:\n  * \"Noise killed Marisse. Or my choice to call signal noise.\"\n  * \"The investigation named it material fatigue. She named it the cost of assuming uncertainty meant safety.\"\n\nAvoid indulgent grief monologues. Her guilt is real but channeled into stricter process.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Dialogue Style for Tiffani\n\nWhen Tiffani speaks:\n\n* **More words than Jhace, but still efficient.**\n\n* **Often clarifying or reframing:**\n  * \"It isn't a pastime. The pattern has repeated across more than a full set of Tides.\"\n\n* **Uses conditional and consequence language:**\n  * \"If it continues, it will affect long-range lenses, signal mirrors, tower fires.\"\n\n* **Slight dry humor when she feels safe:**\n  * \"People who notice patterns get tied to impossible mentors.\"\n  * \"Watching is easier if you don't fall over.\"\n  * With Pella specifically, measurement-based\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765860342.078225,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "baac5781-7fb0-484c-8fc9-04cc0ada52de",

    "bceb353f-4c5c-4842-a0f2-b90f9fc8c019": {

      "message": {
        "author": {
          "metadata": {},
          "name": "python",
          "role": "tool"
        },
        "channel": null,
        "content": {
          "content_type": "execution_output",
          "text": "1 Jhace (tight third) ['Bram', 'Ends', 'Establishes', 'First', 'Jhace', 'Not', 'Opens']\n2 None ['Atmospheric', 'Exit', 'Marisse', 'Mirrors', 'Not', 'Pella']\n3 Jhace (tight third) ['Chloen', 'First', 'Jhace', 'Opens', 'Second', 'Side', 'Surface']\n4 Jhace (tight third) ['Chloen', 'Conditional', 'Emergency', 'Exit', 'Jhace', 'Power', 'The', 'Wakes']\n5 None ['Decision', 'External', 'Guild', 'Jhace', 'Marisse', 'None', 'Observes', 'Opens', 'Pattern', 'Pella']\n6 Jhace (tight third) ['Crisis', 'Demonstrates', 'Exit', 'First', 'Her', 'His', 'Jhace', 'Still', 'Success']\n7 Corlexi (Form god-vessel, mortal name unknown to self) ['Build', 'Chaos', 'Corlexi', 'Count', 'Crown', 'Data', 'Decision', 'Discovery', 'Elements', 'Establish']\n8 Jhace (tight third) ['Controlled', 'Exit', 'Her', 'His', 'Jhace', 'Proposal', 'Resistance', 'Tension']\n9 None ['Corvin', 'Exit', 'First', 'Her', 'His', 'Jhace', 'None', 'Public', 'She', 'Stable']\n10 Both (alternating perspective within chapter) ['Brief', 'Build', 'Conflict', 'Coordinated', 'Count', 'Crisis', 'Elements', 'Entry', 'Establish', 'Exit']\n11 Both (intimate alternating perspective) ['Commitment', 'Exit', 'Marisse', 'Mutual', 'None', 'Physical', 'Post', 'Recognition', 'Trauma']\n12 Both (balanced perspective) ['Acceptance', 'Across', 'After', 'All', 'Anneal', 'Any', 'Approaching', 'Arc', 'Authority', 'Beat']\n"
        },
        "create_time": 1765797101.7381523,
        "end_turn": null,
        "id": "bceb353f-4c5c-4842-a0f2-b90f9fc8c019",
        "metadata": {
          "aggregate_result": {
            "code": "for ch in range(1,13):\n    txt=sections[f\"CH{ch:02d}\"]\n    # find POV line\n    m=re.search(r'- \\*\\*POV:\\*\\* ([^\\n]+)', txt)\n    pov=m.group(1).strip() if m else None\n    # extract names in summary perhaps\n    summary=txt.split(\"**Summary:**\",1)[1] if \"**Summary:**\" in txt else \"\"\n    # list capitalized names occurrences\n    names=set(re.findall(r'\\b[A-Z][a-z]{2,}\\b', summary))\n    # filter some stopwords\n    stop={\"Summary\",\"Key\",\"Beats\",\"Soulpulse\",\"Stage\",\"Progression\",\"Cost\",\"Visuals\",\"Requirements\",\"Relationship\",\"Status\",\"Piercing\",\"Underface\",\"Saint\",\"Merro\",\"Tide\",\"Pouring\",\"Shaping\",\"Striking\",\"Quench\",\"Kindling\",\"Act\"}\n    names=[n for n in names if n not in stop]\n    print(ch, pov, sorted(names)[:10])\n",
            "end_time": 1765797101.83646,
            "final_expression_output": null,
            "in_kernel_exception": null,
